---
title: "Xinerama"
date: 2006-11-17
forum: Multimedia &amp; Video
---

### Post by Ziox on 2006-11-17
[B][SIZE=4]Dual Monitor Support With Xinerama HowTo:

[/SIZE][/B]     [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]The following HowTo attempts to enable Dual Monitor support by utilizing the Xinerama function built into Xorg. [/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT]**[FONT=Verdana][SIZE=3]System Requirements:[/SIZE][/FONT]**[/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]A Dual-Output Graphics card
[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Two Monitors (works best if they are the same resolution, but different resolutions WILL work)[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]A recent version of Xorg.
****[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][B][FONT=Verdana][SIZE=3]Now get ready for some hardcore copy and pasting ;-).

[/SIZE][/FONT][/B][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]1. Open up Xorg.conf using ONE of the following commands:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Gnome (Ubuntu):[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT]```
gksudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]KDE (Kubuntu):[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT]```
kdesu kate /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Xfce4 (Xubuntu):[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT]```
gksudo mousepad /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]Command-Line Based:[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT]```
sudo nano /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```[/LEFT]
        [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]2. ***[COLOR=Red]Navigate to the "Device" Section, and copy the entire Section. Paste what we just copied to the end of the "EndSection" line. (essentially duplicating the entire Section)[/COLOR]***[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT]**[FONT=Verdana]Example:[/FONT]**[/LEFT]
```
[LEFT]Section "Device"
Identifier    "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)
"Driver        "ati"
BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"  

EndSection[/LEFT]
        [LEFT][B]Section "Device"
Identifier    "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
Driver        "ati"
BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection[/B][/LEFT]

```[LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]3. Now we edit the 'Identifiers' for both sections so that they would be different from each other:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][/LEFT]
```
[LEFT]Section "Device"
** Identifier    "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"**
Driver        "ati"
BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"  
EndSection[/LEFT]
        [LEFT]Section "Device"
** Identifier    "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"**
Driver        "ati"
BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"[/LEFT]
EndSection
```    [LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]4. Next, we have to add one "Screen #" to each section. So the result would look something like this:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][/LEFT]
```
[LEFT]Section "Device"
Identifier    "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
Driver        "ati"
BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"
** Screen      0**
EndSection[/LEFT]
        [LEFT]Section "Device"
Identifier    "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
Driver        "ati"
BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"
** Screen      1**
EndSection[/LEFT]

```[INDENT][LEFT][FONT=Verdana]**The following lines are recommended to be added to BOTH Device Sections, however, some of them may be unnecessary.**
[/FONT]```
[B]Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" [/B]#replace primary monitor with the following:
Ati Driver:
NONE   -- Not connected
CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

i810 Driver:
CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
LFP -- Local Flat Panel
DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
TV
CRT2
LFP2
DFP2
TV2
NONE -- Not connected
```[COLOR=Blue][B]NOTE: The following sub-steps are ONLY for people who would like to enable Xinerama on TWO DIFFERENT graphics cards.

4.1. Execute the following command in terminal:
[/B][/COLOR]```
 lspci -X | grep VGA
```[COLOR=Blue][B]
If you have two graphics cards connected, then that command should return two lines like the example below:
[/B][/COLOR]```
 **PCI:1:5:0** ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)
[COLOR=Red]**PCI:1:2:0**[/COLOR] VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation 82810E DC-133 CGC [Chipset Graphics Controller]
```[COLOR=Blue][B]

4.2 Now notice the PCI number returned by that command. What the PCI number does is tell the computer where the graphics card is located. That information is represented in your Xorg.conf file as the "BusID" Since you have TWO graphics cards, then there must be TWO separate "BusID" lines. [/B][/COLOR]

[COLOR=Blue]**4.3 So, replace ONE of the BusID with what we just copied. **[/COLOR]
Example:
```
[LEFT]Section "Device"
Identifier    "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
Driver        "ati"
** BusID        "PCI:1:5:0"**
 Screen      0
EndSection[/LEFT]
        [LEFT]Section "Device"
Identifier    "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
Driver        "ati"
** BusID        [COLOR=Red]"PCI:1:2:0"[/COLOR]**
 Screen      1
EndSection[/LEFT]

```[COLOR=Blue]**Note: For simplicities' sake, do NOT change the Identifier for the Second card. The Identifier is just used for match, what it actually SAYS does NOT matter**[/COLOR].

[COLOR=Blue]**4.4. Follow the rest of the guide.**[/COLOR]
[/LEFT]
[/INDENT][LEFT]*[COLOR=Red]**[FONT=Verdana]5. Now, repeat steps 2 and 3 for the following Sections (if confused, check my completed xorg.conf file at the bottom of the page):[/FONT]**[/COLOR]*[/LEFT]
[LIST]
[*]**"Monitor"**
[*]** "Screen"**[/LIST][LEFT]*[FONT=Verdana][COLOR=Red]**6. In both of the "Screen" Sections, change the Device and Monitor line to match the corresponding Identifiers when changed in previous steps:** [/COLOR][/FONT]*[/LEFT]
    [LEFT]**[FONT=Verdana]Example:[/FONT]**[/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana] ```
Section "Screen"
    [B]Identifier    "Main Screen"
    Device        "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
    Monitor        "Main Monitor"[/B]
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
# Subsections for other display depths not shown...
EndSection
 
Section "Screen"
    [B]Identifier    "Second Screen"
    Device        "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 330M/340M/350M (RS200 IGP)"
    Monitor        "Second Monitor"[/B]
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
# Subsections for other display depths not shown...
EndSection
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]7. Next, locate the "ServerLayout" Section, and edit the Screen line to match the identifier for your main monitor:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    **Screen      0  "Main Screen"**
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
        [LEFT][COLOR=Red]**[FONT=Verdana]8. We need to add another Screen line similar to mine, but make sure that the "Second Screen" and "Main Screen" matches your identifiers for you "Screen" sections that we edited in step 2 to 5:[/FONT]**[/COLOR][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    Screen        0   "Main Screen"
    **Screen       1   "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"**
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][COLOR=Red]***[FONT=Verdana]9. Finally, in order to enable Xinerama when Xorg startup, add the following line to the "ServerLayout" Section:[/FONT]***[/COLOR][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][FONT=Verdana]```
Option "Xinerama" "true"
```[/FONT][/LEFT]
    [LEFT][COLOR=Red][B][I][FONT=Verdana]10. Save the file, and restarted X (ctrl+alt+backspace), or reboot.

[/FONT][/I][/B][/COLOR][/LEFT]
        [LEFT]**[FONT=Verdana]You now should have Dual Monitor enabled.  If not, then please post your problem, and I'm sure someone will help.[/FONT]**[/LEFT]

---

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"

	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"

	Load	"int10"

	Load	"vbe"

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

		Depth		24

EndSection

	Identifier	"1 Default Screen"
	Device		"1 NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"

	SubSection "Display"

	EndSubSection

		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen	      1 "Main Monitor"
	Screen        0 "Second Monitor" RightOf "Main Monitor"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by unlotto on 2006-11-23
> **Figgy said:**
> I followed this best I could, but it didn't work. Here is my xorg.conf file, have a look and tell me what I should do.


Maybe you'd want to try the proprietary nvidia drivers (nv has no 3d accel). If your card is in actuality a dual head card - you'd want twinview instead of xinerama

---

### Post by Figgy on 2006-11-23
I know that I could use twinview using Nvidia's drivers. But since i have 2 geforce 6600 gt's ( one DVI and one VGA plug per card. ) I would like to be able to use one card per monitor versus having one of the cards laying in the computer useless.

### Post by rickdorey on 2006-11-25
the multimonitor configuration worked perfectly.  I'm re-visiting Linux after a few years away.  gotta get my typing precision up to standards again, I did get caught in sloppy naming -- display , monitor , side main etc etc. 
thanks for the advice, again, I'm up with 2 and that was the goal!:)

### Post by dlehman on 2006-11-27
I have a Toshiba satellite, I think its a 2400 series been running xp hone and in xp I have the option of the second monitor showing the same thing as the LCD or extending my desktop to see something different.  I use the extended desktop for presentation so I can see whats coming up and they just see the slide.  

any way I setup dual boot and installed edgy last night when I connect to a CRT I hit fn+F5 and can cycle through LCD, CRT, LCD+CRT, TV all works fine but the LCD+CRT shows the same thing I want the extended desktop for presentations.  I followed this guide and now when I startup I only have one screen and fn+F5 no longer works.  and one more minor issue I'm not to worried about, in xp I had LCD+TV with different resolution 

any suggestions?
here is my xorg.conf
> 
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

# again, run the following command:

Section "Files"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts

EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"

	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"

	Load	"vbe"

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"

	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"

EndSection

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"

	Option "DDCMode" "True"

		Modes		"1024x768"

	EndSubSection

		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"1 Default Screen"
	Device		"1 S3 Inc. SuperSavage IX/C SDR"
	Monitor		"1 Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	16
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "0 Default Screen"
	Screen		1 "1 Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection


thanks in advance

### Post by Ziox on 2006-11-27
> **dlehman said:**
> I have a Toshiba satellite, I think its a 2400 series been running xp hone and in xp I have the option of the second monitor showing the same thing as the LCD or extending my desktop to see something different.  I use the extended desktop for presentation so I can see whats coming up and they just see the slide.  

any way I setup dual boot and installed edgy last night when I connect to a CRT I hit fn+F5 and can cycle through LCD, CRT, LCD+CRT, TV all works fine but the LCD+CRT shows the same thing I want the extended desktop for presentations.  I followed this guide and now when I startup I only have one screen and fn+F5 no longer works.  and one more minor issue I'm not to worried about, in xp I had LCD+TV with different resolution 

any suggestions?
here is my xorg.conf



thanks in advance

Change this line at the very end of the file:
```
Screen 1 "1 Default Screen"
```
to:
```
Screen "1 Default Screen" RightOf "0 Default Screen"
```
you can change the 'RightOf' part to either leftof, above, or below, based on the physical placement of the monitors.

---

### Post by dlehman on 2006-11-27
I apologize  for making such a simple error I thought I had that in there. Thanks a lot

### Post by TheCat on 2006-11-27
I've got an ATI Radeon 9000 Pro (VGA and DVI outputs) and two Samsung SyncMaster 710N 17" LCD panels.  I'm using the fglrx driver.  Xinerama seems to be working fine, but on the secondary monitor, the mouse cursor won't change -- it's totally static as whatever it was displaying when I moved it from the primary screen to the secondary screen.  For example, most of the time, it's a regular arrow, and in Firefox, it won't change to a hand.  When resizing a window, it won't change to a targeting cursor.  It stays an arrow.  If it happened to be displayed as a hand when I moved it to the secondary screen, it continues to display as a hand.  The cursor also seems to 'jump' a bit when it hits transition points, like the edge of a window.  This is more of an annoyance than a serious problem (it's a bit disconcerting), but I wondered if anyone had encountered it or could suggest a solution.

Here's my xorg.conf:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon RV250 If [Radeon 9000 Pro]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon RV250 If [Radeon 9000 Pro]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		1
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"0 SyncMaster"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"1 SyncMaster"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"0 Default Screen"

	Monitor		"0 SyncMaster"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1

	EndSubSection

	Screen 0	"0 Default Screen"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

### Post by AntiMattR on 2006-11-30
First off, the HOW TO is awesome, thanks!

I have a Radeon X1600 with dual LCDs.  Hardware OpenGL was working previously.

Ideas??

This is the xorg.conf. Can you see what I have done wrong. 

```
Section "ServerFlags"
         Option "xinerama"
         Option "DefaultServerLayout" "Multihead"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"

	Identifier	"multihead"
        Option "xinerama" "true"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
        Screen         "Screen Right" rightof "Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Default Screen" 0 0
  	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"

EndSection

	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"

	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
	Option		"XkbVariant"	"dvorak"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"

	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

EndSection

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY

	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
        Screen 0
        Option "TwinView" "0"
        Option "Metamodes" "1280x1024,1280x1024; 1280x960,1280x960; 1152x864,1152x864; 800x600,800x600; 1024x768,1024x768; 1280x1024,NULL; 1024x768,NULL"

### Post by unlotto on 2006-12-04
> 
My version of your code is giving a fatal server error. It seems not to like the same pci express card being addressed as if it was two devices.

Any comments appreciated, especially as I can not really take the jump to Ub without 2 monitors working in Xinerama mode.

if you have one card, use twinview instead of xinerama (not both as you're trying to  I believe it outperforms xinerama.

You need only one device with dualheaded twinview as well as one screen

```

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NV41 [GeForce 6800?] #1"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"S/T 97P/96P"
	Option		"DPMS"
	 HorizSync   30 - 96.0
	 VertRefresh 50-160


EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV41 [GeForce 6800?] #1"
	Monitor		"S/T 97P/96P"
        Option "CoolBits" "1"
        Option "TwinView" "True"
        Option "TwinViewOrientation" "RightOf"
        Option "UseEdidFreqs" "True"
        Option "MetaModes" "1400x1050, 1400x1050"

	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		 "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		 "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		 "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		 "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		 "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		 "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Main" 0 0
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

```

---

### Post by TheCat on 2006-12-05
> if you have one card, use twinview instead of xinerama (not both as you're trying to I believe it outperforms xinerama.

You need only one device with dualheaded twinview as well as one screen

Note that this advice only applies because it's an nvidia card.  It won't work with, for example, my ATI Radeon 9000 PRO.

---

### Post by Robbyx on 2006-12-06
Thanks for that advice. I actually got the Xinerama to work. Now I have messed it because I installed beryl and tried to update the Nv driver. A file was not correctly downloaded in the course of running beryl and at the moment I do not have a GDM.

Robin

### Post by bobo1on1 on 2006-12-06
I have a television connected to the vga port and a tft monitor connected to the dvi port of my geforce FX5500, to get Xinerama to work I had to add

 BusID               "PCI:1:0:0"


to the two device sections.

The only problem is everything is alot slower now, the Xorg process eats alot of cpu, sometimes as high as 80%, and around 20% when idle.

Twinview is no option for me because the firefox menu's don't show up in the right place because of the low television resolution.

---

### Post by Robbyx on 2006-12-06
Above I said I had installed Beryl. That is wrong I had used Envy to install the new Nvidia driver and something went wrong with the installation and it aborted. I am left with no way into the Graphic mode of Ubuntu. I have brought this up in another topic in the hope that someone will help me out.

Robin

### Post by wjbaird on 2006-12-11
I have a Dell D410 latop with an Intel I915 graphics chipset.  I just upgraded from 6.06LTS to 6.10.  I had Xinerama working perfectly with 6.06, but it is now broken in a strange way. 

The external monitor is being activated, and I can see things on it, but for some reason all windows open on the internal monitor, and I can't completely drag them onto the external monitor - it always leaves maybe 50-100 pixels on the laptop screen.

Equally strange, I run a few windows apps using cross-over office, and if I try to resize any of the windows apps to be larger than 1024x768 (the internal montior's resolution) the area outside of 1024x768 doesn't refresh at all.  I don't know if this is related or not.

I verified my xorg.conf, and the only thing that the upgrade process changed was to remove "GLCore" from the list of extensions to load.  I tried adding it back in, and it didn't help at all.

Any advice would be welcome...

---

EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by Ziox on 2006-12-11
> **giruzz said:**
> Hi,

I followed almost step-by-step the how-to but it doesn't work for me (that why I wrote 'almost')...

This is my xorg.conf, when I'm using this conf file all I obtain is a black screen then I have to reboot in 'recovery' mode and use my xorg.con_backup

anyone can help me?

thanks

g.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    # path to defoma fonts
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load    "i2c"
    Load    "bitmap"
    Load    "ddc"
    Load    "dri"
    Load    "extmod"
    Load    "freetype"
    Load    "glx"
    Load    "int10"
    Load    "type1"
    Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "CoreKeyboard"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
    Option        "XkbOptions"    "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
    Option        "Protocol"        "ExplorerPS/2"
    Option        "ZAxisMapping"        "4 5"
    Option        "Emulate3Buttons"    "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Driver        "synaptics"
#    Option        "SendCoreEvents"    "true"
#    Option        "Device"        "/dev/psaux"
#    Option        "Protocol"        "auto-dev"
#    Option        "HorizScrollDelta"    "0"
    Option        "TouchpadOff"        "1"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "0 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
    Driver        "i810"
    BusID        "PCI:0:2:0"
    Screen        0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "1 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
    Driver        "i810"
    BusID        "PCI:0:2:0"
    Screen        1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Main Monitor"
    Option        "DPMS"
    HorizSync    28-51
    VertRefresh    43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Second Monitor"
    Option        "DPMS"
    HorizSync    28-51
    VertRefresh    43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Main Screen"
    Device        "0 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
    Monitor        "Main Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Second Screen"
    Device        "1 Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
    Monitor        "Second Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1024x768"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    Screen          0  "Main Screen"
    Screen        1   "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
    Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection

```

You almost have everything.  Adding the line:

```
Option "MonitorLayout" "primary display, secondary display"
```

to both device sections.
Refer to step 4 of the guide for the value replacements.

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "ScreenRight"
	Screen		1 "ScreenLeft" LeftOf "ScreenRight"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

### Post by Ziox on 2006-12-20
I would suggest not to just copy everything.  You have to understand why you are adding whatever you are adding.  For example, for the

Option "MonitorLayout"

You need to replace "primary" and "secondary" monitor with the values I have given you.  If you need help, check other people's Xorg file.

Another thing is to follow directions.  IN the ServeryLayout Section, apply the following changes:

Screen 1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"

change rightof to either "above", "below", leftof

#	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Any help would be appreciated.
Thanks :)


P.S: I'm running edgy. I've tested it on Feisty with no luck :(

### Post by elektronaut on 2007-01-04
Pheew... I finally managed it, thanks to copy & paste. First I tried [this xorg.conf](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1659471&postcount=24), which gave me some vbe initialization error. [This xorg.conf](http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1823241&postcount=3) finally worked, although I had to reboot in order to avoid the vbe error and also the external monitor has to be plugged out during the boot process, otherwise Xserver crashes. Don't forget to change the sync rates and resolution modes according to your display specs if you want to try these files yourself.
I didn't need to tweak the video bios with 915resolution anymore. That is apparently only needed if you use modelines?

---

### Post by TeasesTheBear on 2007-02-03
> **joris1977 said:**
> Ok, take 2. When i try to load this xorg, it doesn´t work: the log says WW I810 No Matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI: 0:2:1 found  
it also says: EE I810 (o) monitor 1 and 2 cannot be type None.
 

Had The same problem on an IBM ThinkCentre with the (I think, posting this from another machine) 915G integrated chip form Intel (using the i810 dirver though) and a PCI-card extension for a second head from IBM/Lenovo.
integrated chip: PCI:0:2:0
extension card: PCI:0:2:1
I'm using two (always connected) analog TFTs, which have to be addressed, because of their analog nature, as CRTs.

Now, here's what wasn't stated too clear and solved my problem:
1.) one has to replace "primary monitor" AND "secondary monitor" with the monitor type in the MonitorLayouts
2.) there must be NO space after the comma in the MonitorLayoutOption
3.) the second CRT is not called CRT2 but Secondary

This makes my MonitorLayouts look like this:
```

"MonitorLayout" "CRT,Secondary"

```

The space causes the "EE I810 (o) monitor 1 and 2 cannot be type None" error, since the driver seems to interpret values starting with a space as NONE, i.e. not connected.

A wrong or unknown Value for a monitortype results in the "WW I810 No Matching Device section for instance (BusID PCI: 0:2:1) found" error. Meaning the driver found a device (in my case "Secondary") and could not find any proper definition/device section for it (of corse not, we told it to look for "CRT2").

Hint: if you are unsure wahat type of monitor you have, connect your Monitor(s), (re)start X and have a look at your /var/log/Xorg.0.log
It's pretty huge, but there is a section, where the driver attempts to find any possible monitortype it knows of, and tells you what types it found.

Now, after numerous trial and error attempts and hours of reading the Xorg.0.log I'm finally rocking on Ubuntu with two screens at work.:guitar:
 Ahhh, soo good not beeing forced to use Windows anymore:)

### Post by ToastBusters on 2007-02-12
Okay, good news. I figured out what my problem was. I needed to have my BusID value in the device sections. It works like a charm now :)

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-14
> **bushblows said:**
> I have been trying to configure Dual monitors on a Dell Inspiron B130 with an Intel Corporation Mobile 915GM/GMS/910GML Express Graphics Controller for most of the last 24 hours. 


A measly 24 hours? Took me days!

Okay.  First off, are you restarting X with Ctl+Alt+Bksp?  If so, I found that this only worked sometimes.  About half the time I had to do a full reboot.  Dunno why.

Specific stuff I found out that is relevant to  your xorg.conf:

```
Section "Device"
  identifier "0 Intel Corporation Mobile 915GM/GMS/910GML Express Graphics Controller"
  boardname "i810"
  busid "PCI:0:2:0"
  driver "i810"
  screen 0
  Option "DDCMode" "True"

Option "MonitorLayout" "DFP, DFP"
EndSection

```

The second pipe for a latitude d520 in the MonitorLayout is always the  LCD screen.

```
Section "Screen"
  Identifier "Main Screen"
  Device "Intel Corporation Mobile 915GM/GMS/910GML Express Graphics Controller"
  Monitor "Main Monitor"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    depth 24
    modes "1024x768@60" "1280x1024@60" "800x600@60" "1600x1200@60" "640x480@60" "1792x1344@60" "1856x1392@60" "1920x1440@60"
  EndSubSection
EndSection

```

The Device identifier in your screen sections doesn't match the identifiers you have used in the Device sections. (This could be the majority of the problem).

```
Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Main Screen"
  screen 1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
  InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
  InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
  InputDevice "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
  InputDevice "Synaptics Touchpad"
  Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

```

For some reason, the i810 driver doesn't seem to like the numbers after the screen in the serverlayout section.  It only works if they are both 0 or not there.  (Don't ask me why!)

I add  the option "clone" "false" to this section and the serverflags section.  It shouldn't be required but...

```
Section "device" #   
  identifier "device1"
  boardname "i810"
  busid "PCI:0:2:0"
  driver "i810"
  screen 1
EndSection
Section "screen" #   
  identifier "screen1"
  device "device1"
  defaultdepth 24
  monitor "monitor1"
  SubSection "Display"
    depth 24
    modes "640x480@60"
  EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "monitor" #   
  identifier "monitor1"
  vendorname "Plug 'n' Play"
  modelname "Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  gamma 1.0
EndSection
Section "device" #   
  identifier "device2"
  boardname "VESA driver (generic)"
  busid "PCI:0:2:1"
  driver "vesa"
  screen 0
EndSection
Section "screen" #   
  identifier "screen2"
  device "device2"
  defaultdepth 24
  monitor "monitor2"
  SubSection "Display"
    depth 24
    modes "640x480@60" "800x600@56" "800x600@60"
  EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "monitor" #   
  identifier "monitor2"
  vendorname "Generic"
  modelname "Flat Panel 800x600"
  HorizSync 31.5-37.9
  VertRefresh 40-70
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  gamma 1.0
EndSection


```

This stuff at the end of your file may be getting in the way.  Save it somewhere else!

Hope this helps.

---

### Post by jis on 2007-02-15
bushblows, for me it worked better, when the laptop's own LCD is LFP, not DFP, in the Option "MonitorLayout" row. I wonder where you get that modeline info. Should it be used with an external CRT, too?

---

### Post by jis on 2007-02-15
How can you choose in boot time which xorg.conf to use? You may want to use different xorg.conf, when an external screen has been connected to a notebook PC...

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-15
> **jis said:**
> How can you choose in boot time which xorg.conf to use? You may want to use different xorg.conf, when an external screen has been connected to a notebook PC...

Nothing simple sorry. I have three avenues I intend following up:

[LIST=1]
[*]Something called scmp.  It's part of the suse distro, can't find anything like it in Ubuntu currently.

[*]A set of scripts for doing this: [http://www.fmi.uni-passau.de/~zimmerma/xlayout_chooser/](http://www.fmi.uni-passau.de/~zimmerma/xlayout_chooser/)

[*]Someone else had the idea a set of scripts based around starting up at runlevel 3, then starting X from a different tty based on what config they wanted.
[/LIST]

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-16
Interesting problem.  Can you please post your xorg.conf file?

I understood that pipe B was always the LCD screen, but that doesn't seem consistent with your log output.

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-17
> **surhudm said:**
> I have already posted my xorg.conf above isnt it?


Sorry, missed that.

Okay, two things for sure:

[LIST=1]
[*]Use the same pci address in both device sections.
[*]Remove the numbers (0 and 1) in the screen lines  of the serverlayout section
[/LIST]
Also, I think you need to change the monitorlayout to "CRT,LFP"

Thanks for the help.

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-17
Here's my xorg.conf in case it helps. 

```
##########################################################################################
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This version by: Kerry Mayes, February 2007.
# For Dell Latitude D520 with external Viewsonic VE710b Monitor connected via docking station.
# 
# As an edited file, it will not be automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades. 
#
# I have not run the following command to allow it to be automatically updated:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
##########################################################################################
#
# These sections have not been altered.
Section "Files"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
       FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
       # path to defoma fonts
       FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
       Load    "i2c"
       Load    "bitmap"
       Load    "ddc"
       Load    "dri"
       Load    "extmod"
       Load    "freetype"
       Load    "glx"
       Load    "int10"
       Load    "type1"
       Load    "vbe"
       Load    "dbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
       Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
       Driver          "kbd"
       Option          "CoreKeyboard"
       Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
       Option          "XkbModel"      "pc104"
       Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
       Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
       Driver          "mouse"
       Option          "CorePointer"
       Option          "Device"                "/dev/input/mice"
       Option          "Protocol"              "ExplorerPS/2"
       Option          "ZAxisMapping"          "4 5"
       Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
       Identifier      "Synaptics Touchpad"
       Driver          "synaptics"
       Option          "SendCoreEvents"        "true"
       Option          "Device"                "/dev/psaux"
       Option          "Protocol"              "auto-dev"
       Option          "HorizScrollDelta"      "0"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# First device section, will control the Laptop LCD screen
Section "Device"
       Identifier      "D520 945GM 0"
       Driver "i810"
       BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
       # This next line is required for xinerama.
       Screen 0
       # It was stated somewhere that the LFP is always the second pipe. Make sure never 
       # to have a space after the "," it's interpreted as "None". 
       Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
       # I don't think this next line is actually required.
		 Option "DevicePresence" "on"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# Second device section, will control the external screen 
Section "Device"
       Identifier "D520 945GM 1"
       Driver "i810"
		 # Even though there is a second pci address for the card, only this one is used for 
		 # the external monitor.  The other may be for the svideo out but that has not been
		 # confirmed.
       BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
       # This next line is required for xinerama.
       Screen 1
       # It was stated somewhere that the LFP is always the second pipe. Make sure never 
       # to have a space after the "," it's interpreted as "None". 
       Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
       # I don't think this next line is actually required.
		 Option "DevicePresence" "on"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# First Monitor section, for the Laptop LCD screen.
Section "Monitor"
       Identifier      "D520 Monitor"
       # I can't find a definitive source for these values, so am not using them.
		 # HorizSync       30-64
       # VertRefresh     48-120
       DisplaySize     304 228     # From manual
       Option          "DPMS"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# Second Monitor section, for the Viewsonic monitor connected to the docking station.
Section "Monitor"
       Identifier "Viewsonic VE710b Monitor"
       # These values are from the monitor's manual.
       HorizSync       30-82
       VertRefresh     50-75
       DisplaySize	338 270
       Option "DPMS"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# First screen section, connects the first device to the Laptop LCD screen.
# Note that the resolution "1400x1050" wasn't available until I had installed 915resolution
Section "Screen"
       Identifier      "D520 Screen"
       Device          "D520 945GM 0"
       Monitor         "D520 Monitor"
       DefaultDepth    24
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth           24
               Modes           "1400x1050" "1280x1024"
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth           16
               Modes           "1400x1050" "1280x1024"
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# Second screen section, connects the second device to the Viewsonic monitor connected to 
# the docking station.
Section "Screen"
       Identifier "Viewsonic VE710b Screen"
       Device "D520 945GM 1"
       Monitor "Viewsonic VE710b Monitor"
       DefaultDepth 24
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth 24
               Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768"
               # This next line has been found useful when xinerama automatically makes 
               # the two screens the same virtual size - this command can be used on the 
               # lower resolution screen to limit it to the resolution it can display 
               # without scrolling.
               # virtual 1280 1024
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth 16
               Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768"
               # This next line has been found useful when xinerama automatically makes 
               # the two screens the same virtual size - this command can be used on the 
               # lower resolution screen to limit it to the resolution it can display 
               # without scrolling.
               # virtual 1280 1024
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# The serverlayout section to put it all together.  
# Currently I use this file for both Laptop only and Dual screens so have to comment things 
# out or put them back in each time I switch.
#
Section "ServerLayout"
       Identifier      "Default Layout"
       # For the Intel, having a screen number in these lines seems to get it confused.
       Screen       "D520 Screen" 0 0
       # Comment out the next two lines to use just the laptop screen.        
#       Screen       "Viewsonic VE710b Screen" LeftOf "D520 Screen"
#       Option "Xinerama" "on"
       Option "Clone" "off"
       InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
       InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
       InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
#
# Sometimes gnome remembers layout stuff that screws up the screen - you can't drag windows
# into certain parts of the screen and so forth.  The solution I found on the forums was to
# delete the following directory: [home directory]/.gconf/desktop/gnome/screen
#
#
##########################################################################################
#
# These sections not altered.
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
       Mode    0666
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################

```

---

After many, many attempts at this, I finally ended up back where I started; I have a modified xorg.conf file (below) that only displays on the default monitor.

I have a Toshiba Portege M200 tablet PC with an ordinary CRT monitor attached to the secondary output.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

#

Section "Files"

Section "Screen"

	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1

		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen	0	"Screen 0"
	Screen	1	"Screen 1" RightOf "Screen 0"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"

	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Before remembering to put in "CRT," I was either getting two blank displays, or the attached screen would be solid white, then red, then green, then blue, then gray three times, then black, and then would repeat sometimes. Is there anything wrong with my xorg.conf?

I realize I may be getting ahead of myself by asking this, but is there an option I can add to rotate one of the monitors 90 or 180 degrees? Since I have a tablet PC, this option would be nice.

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-19
Here are my suggested changes:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"

	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"

EndSection

	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"

  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Intel 82852/855GM"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	VideoRam	64000
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Option		"DDCMode"		"true"
	Option		"MonitorLayout"		"CRT,LFP2"
	Screen		0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 Intel 82852/855GM"
	Driver		"i810"
# CHANGE 1 - SAME PCI ADDRESS.
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	VideoRam	64000
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Option		"DDCMode"		"true"
	Option		"MonitorLayout"		"CRT,LFP2"
	Screen		1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Monitor 0"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-90
	VertRefresh	50-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Monitor 1"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-90
	VertRefresh	50-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 0"
	Device		"0 Intel 82852/855GM"
	Monitor		"Monitor 0"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1400x1050" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 1"
	Device		"1 Intel 82852/855GM"
	Monitor		"Monitor 1"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
# CHANGE 2 DON'T USE SCREEN NUMBERS FOR i810. (Or make them both 0)
	Screen		"Screen 0"
	Screen		"Screen 1" RightOf "Screen 0"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
# CHANGE 3 - FOR SAFETY
	Option		"Clone" "false"
EndSection

#CHANGE 4 - ALSO FOR SAFETY
Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
	Option		"Clone" "false"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

Yes there is a way to turn the screen around, I'll see if I can find it again.

BTW: Why are you using LFP2 rather than LFP?

	Identifier	"Monitor One"

Section "Screen"

	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection

		Depth		24

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen	0	"Screen Zero"
	Screen	1	"Screen One" LeftOf "Screen Zero"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
	Option		"Clone"			"false"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
	Option		"Clone"			"false"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-20
> **42Wired said:**
> My next question would be how to rotate them independently. When I put my laptop on my docking station, the screen is rotated such that it's upside-down.

Is there an Option "Rotate" or something I can add to it to correct it?


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen	0	"Screen Zero"
	Screen	1	"Screen One" LeftOf "Screen Zero"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
	Option		"Clone"			"false"
EndSection



How bizarre!  When I had the "Screen 0" and "Screen 1" in the server layout section it wouldn't work for me! Anyway, good that it's working.

The following option goes in the device section (I believe), so it should apply just to one monitor (?).
Option "Rotate" "90"
    Rotate the desktop 90 degrees counterclockwise. Other valid options are 0, 90, 180 and 270 degrees. The RandR extension is used for rotation functionality. So this option allows the Xserver to start with a rotated mode of operation. Default: 0 degrees.

One other thing, do you have any issues with dragging windows to various places on the screen?  A number of people get that and you need to delete some gnome stored settings.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-20
> **WhiteStool said:**
> I have tried many things to get this working, and I can't seem to get it. I have checked and double checked my BusIDs, and there's nothing else I can think to do after reading through all these posts. My xorg.conf is below.

For nvidea cards there is a program called twinview that works in conjunction with / instead of xinerama.  By all accounts it is easier to get set up.  I would try that if I were you.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-20
> **WhiteStool said:**
> I have tried many things to get this working, and I can't seem to get it. I have checked and double checked my BusIDs, and there's nothing else I can think to do after reading through all these posts. My xorg.conf is below.

However, if you want to persist with xinerama, here's a couple of ideas:

1. Do you have two video cards or just one?  If just one, you probably want to use the same pci address for each device.

2. Add this option to your serverlayout section.
	Option		"Clone"			"false"

3. Add a server flags section:

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"Xinerama"		"true"
	Option		"Clone"			"false"
EndSection

Otherwise that looks good to me (unless nvidea is much much different - check the man page for the nv driver)

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-24
Twinview is quite different to xinerama, so posting under this topic may not be as much use as you'd like.  

Under xinerama, you need to have two devices, two screens, two monitors, and you specify where everything fits in the serverlayout section.  If you've only added stuff in the device section, you may not have done enough.  I'm sure I saw a twinview howto around here somewhere, see if you can find that.

---

```


# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

EndSection

### Post by jis on 2007-02-25
SourceV, try different types in Option "MonitorLayout" section.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-25
A few suggestions to start with:

[LIST=1]
[*]Doesn't the ati driver provide a different way of implementing dual heads? Twinview is for NVidia and there's something special for ati as well.  Not sure if it's easier or not. Many of the options are driver specific so not sure which, if any, of my suggestions will help.

[*]You have two server layout sections both labeled default!  I think you mean to use the one at the bottom of the file.

[*]I sometimes get the error "Caught signal 11.  Server aborting" after modifying my xorg.conf and restarting X with ctrl+alt+Bksp - it goes away when I restart.

[*]There may be an issue with numbering the devices screen 1 and 2, this may need to be 0 and 1.
[/LIST]

---

### Post by kerry_s on 2007-02-26
Option "SWCursor" "boolean"

    Enable or disable software rendering of the X cursor. Default: off.
Option "HWCursor" "boolean"

    Enable or disable hardware rendering of the X cursor. Default: on.


-> [http://download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html](http://download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html)

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-02-26
> **42Wired said:**
> I'm still not having any luck getting my left monitor to rotate 180 degrees with Xinerama. I was all over Google about it, but I didn't find anything that was of much help.

Are you using ubuntu 6.10?  This link ([http://www.thinkwiki.org/wiki/Installing_Ubuntu_6.10_on_a_ThinkPad_X41_Tablet#Tablet_Screen_Rotation_.28updated_i810_drivers.29](http://www.thinkwiki.org/wiki/Installing_Ubuntu_6.10_on_a_ThinkPad_X41_Tablet#Tablet_Screen_Rotation_.28updated_i810_drivers.29)) indicates that xrandr is part of the i810 driver in edgy.

I tried the command

xrandr --screen 0 -o left 

and it rotated my main screen.  

Only issue was that after rotating it back I got the gnome issue of remembering screen stuff that it shouldn't (can't drag windows into that area etc.

### Post by baky0520 on 2007-02-27
> **kerry_s said:**
> Option "SWCursor" "boolean"

    Enable or disable software rendering of the X cursor. Default: off.
Option "HWCursor" "boolean"

    Enable or disable hardware rendering of the X cursor. Default: on.


-> [http://download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html](http://download.nvidia.com/solaris/1.0-8762/README/appendix-d.html)

problem solved, thanks a lot!


@SourceV:
this works for me:

  # in the external Device Section
  option "SWCursor" "on"
  option "HWCursor" "off"

Sorry, I've been away.  I saw a note indicating that xrandr was new to edgy, but if you've got it in dapper then it shouldn't be a problem. 

Can you rotate / invert screen 0?  

I couldn't find any information  on using the display option.

The can't drag stuff in areas issue is one I got after finally getting the dual heads going.  The desktop wasn't using the full double width desktop properly.  Dragging windows would only allow them to go slightly onto the right hand monitor.  Maximising windows on the right hand screen would result in a tall thin window on the left of the right hand screen.  Maximising on the left hand screen would give a window that extended slightly onto the right hand screen.  Really annoying!  The solution I found on this forum somewhere was to delete the directory I mentioned.

    Identifier    "**0** Intel Corporation Mobile Integrated Graphics Controller"
    Driver        "i810"
    BusID        "PCI:0:2:0"
[B]    Screen        0
    Option        "DDCMode"     "True"
    Option        "MonitorLayout" "LFP,DFP"
    Option        "UseFBDev"    "True"[/B]
EndSection

[B]Section "Device"
    Identifier    "1 Intel Corporation Mobile Integrated Graphics Controller"
    Driver        "i810"
    BusID        "PCI:0:2:0"
    Screen        1
    Option        "DDCMode"     "True"
    Option        "MonitorLayout" "LFP,DFP"
    Option        "UseFBDev"    "True"
EndSection[/B]

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Primary Monitor"
    Option        "DPMS"
EndSection

[B]Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Secondary Monitor"
    Option        "DPMS"
EndSection[/B]

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Screen **0**"
    Device        "**0** Intel Corporation Mobile Integrated Graphics Controller"
    Monitor        "Primary Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

[B]Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Screen 1"
    Device        "1 Intel Corporation Mobile Integrated Graphics Controller"
    Monitor        "Secondary Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1280x800"
    EndSubSection
EndSection[/B]

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    Screen        "Screen 0"
**    Screen        "Screen 1" RightOf "Screen 0"**
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
[B]    Option        "Xinerama"    "true"
    Option        "Clone" "false"[/B]
EndSection
[B]
Section "ServerFlags"
    Option        "Xinerama"    "true"
    Option        "Clone"        "false"
EndSection[/B]

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection


```

### Post by epswing on 2007-03-10
Hookay, I've got a laptop (Toshiba Satellite M40), it's got a VGA port on the side, connected to an lcd (LG Flatron L1952TX).

Here's the xorg.conf file I set up:

```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Main Video Card"
	# above identifier used to be:
	# "Intel Corporation Mobile 915GM/GMS/910GML Express Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,LFP" #replace primary monitor with the following:
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Second Video Card"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,LFP" #replace primary monitor with the following:
EndSection

# options for Option "MonitorLayout" above
#
#Ati Driver:
#NONE   -- Not connected
#CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
#TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
#LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel
#
#i810 Driver:
#CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
#LFP -- Local Flat Panel
#DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
#TV
#CRT2
#LFP2
#DFP2
#TV2
#NONE -- Not connected

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"Main Video Card"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"Second Video Card"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Main Screen"
	Screen		1 "Second Screen" LeftOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection
```

I restarted the X server with CtrlAltBackspace, and it "worked" (went into graphical mode), but it just displayed a blank (black) screen on my laptop lcd (and nothing on the connected lcd).  Anyone see anything wrong with my config file?

**Edit:** The line ```
Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,LFP"
``` was the only part of the guide I found confusing, and I'm not sure if it's correct.

**Edit2:** Also, if I change my ServerLayout to...```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Main Screen"
#	Screen		0 "Main Screen"
#	Screen		1 "Second Screen" LeftOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection
```
...I get the same results.

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
