Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
# 	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0 # this line is commented out
	Screen      0  "Main Screen"
	Screen      1  "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Main Monitor"
	Identifier   "Main Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Second Monitor"
        Identifier   "Second Monitor"
        Option      "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility X700 (RV410 PCIE)"
	Driver      "vesa"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      0 
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS" 
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility X700 (RV410 PCIE)"
        Driver      "vesa"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"	
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS" 
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Main Screen"
	Device     "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility X700 (RV410 PCIE)"
	Monitor    "Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Second Screen"
        Device     "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility X700 (RV410 PCIE)"
        Monitor    "Second Monitor"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     1
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     4
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     8
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     15
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     16
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     24

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection



```

    BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen      0 
    Option "DDCMode" "True"
    Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS"

        Driver      "vesa"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen      1
    Option "DDCMode" "True"    
    Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS" 
EndSection

    Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
    Driver      "fglrx"
    Option        "VideoOverlay" "on"
    Option        "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
EndSection
 
Section "Screen"
    Identifier "Main Screen"
    Device     "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility X700 (RV410 PCIE)"
    Monitor    "Main Monitor"
    DefaultDepth     24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth     1
        Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth     4
        Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth     8
        Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth     15
        Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth     16
        Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth     24
        Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection
 
Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Second Screen"
        Device     "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Mobility X700 (RV410 PCIE)"
        Monitor    "Second Monitor"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     1
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     4
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     8
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     15
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     16
                Modes    "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth     24
 
Section "Screen"
    Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
    Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
    Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
    DefaultDepth     24
    SubSection "Display"
        Viewport   0 0
        Depth     24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
 
Section "DRI"
    Mode         0666
EndSection
 
Section "Extensions"
    Option        "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
 
 

```
 
I would advice you to change the driver for your Ati graphic card to either "fglrx" or "ati" or "radeon".  Also if possible, add the Horizontal and vertical sync rate in the "Montior" Sections.

### Post by wankel on 2007-03-19
After some fiddling with xorg.conf, I got a working set for my setup. I post it here mainly for two reasons:
- helping others with a similar setup
- possibly have someone shed a light on an issue that remains.

1. The peculiar thing about my setup is that is has somewhat older cards: two Matrox Millenium 2MB cards, with an extra 2MB addon card making 4 meg per card.
My problem was that my computer would freeze on starting X after booting my pc normally. On configuring, there would be no problem.
I have found the solution in booting in recovery mode (that is, just making sure X does not get started). Then I run "X -configure", just for the fun of my second card getting initiated.
After that I can enter runlevel 5 (telinit 5) without a problem.

2. I am running two monitors at 1600*1200, but I would like to turn them to portrait, as many of you in this tread are trying as well. 
I understand from the conversation so far, that "Option" "Rotate" 270 is not an option as long as Xinerama is "true" as well. 
Does anyone of you have experience with a trick or card that will do this? I am planning to upgrade my graphic cards to something with DVI on it, being two PCI cards or one AGP with dual DVI. Any succestions?

Here's my xorg.conf; I used to run it in combination with an AGP card for a third screen. Though it is more of the same, if anyone is interested I will post it.

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
  FontPath "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc  	"
  # path to defoma fonts
  FontPath "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
  Load "i2c"
  Load "bitmap"
  Load "ddc"
  Load "extmod"
  Load "freetype"
  Load "int10"
  Load "type1"
  Load "vbe"
  #  load "GLcore"
  #  load "v4l"
  load "glx"
  load "GLcore"
  load "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Generic Keyboard"
  Driver "kbd"
  option "CoreKeyboard"
  option "XkbRules" "xorg"
  option "XkbModel" "pc105"
  option "XkbLayout" "ch"
  option "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
  #  option "XkbVariant" "de"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Identifier "Configured Mouse"
  Driver "mouse"
  option "CorePointer"
  option "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
  option "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
  option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
  option "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
  identifier "mill-boven"
  boardname "mga"
  busid "PCI:2:6:0"
  driver "mga"
  screen 0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  identifier "ctx"
  modelname "Custom 1"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1152x768@54" 64.995 1152 1178 1314 1472 768 771 777 806 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x854" 80.0 1280 1309 1460 1636 854 857 864 896 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1400x1050@60" 122.61 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1600x1200@60" 162.0 1600 1664 1856 2160 1200 1201 1204 1250 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1792x1344@60" 204.8 1792 1920 2120 2448 1344 1345 1348 1394 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1856x1392@60" 218.3 1856 1952 2176 2528 1392 1393 1396 1439 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1920x1440@60" 234.0 1920 2048 2256 2600 1440 1441 1444 1500 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "2048x1536@60" 266.95 2048 2200 2424 2800 1536 1537 1540 1589 -hsync +vsync
  gamma 1.0
EndSection

Section "screen"
  Identifier "links"
  Device "mill-boven"
  Monitor "ctx"
  DefaultDepth 24
  SubSection "Display"
    depth 24
    modes "1600x1200@60" "1792x1344@60" "1400x1050@60" "1856x1392@60" "1280x1024@60" "1920x1440@60" "1280x960@60" "2048x1536@60" "1280x854" "1152x768@54" "1024x768@60" "800x600@60" "800x600@56" "640x480@60"
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "device"
  identifier "mill-onder"
  boardname "mga"
  busid "PCI:2:7:0"
  driver "mga"
  screen 0
EndSection

Section "monitor"
  identifier "agneovo"
  modelname "Custom 2"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1152x768@54" 64.995 1152 1178 1314 1472 768 771 777 806 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x854" 80.0 1280 1309 1460 1636 854 857 864 896 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1400x1050@60" 122.61 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1600x1200@60" 162.0 1600 1664 1856 2160 1200 1201 1204 1250 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1792x1344@60" 204.8 1792 1920 2120 2448 1344 1345 1348 1394 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1856x1392@60" 218.3 1856 1952 2176 2528 1392 1393 1396 1439 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1920x1440@60" 234.0 1920 2048 2256 2600 1440 1441 1444 1500 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "2048x1536@60" 266.95 2048 2200 2424 2800 1536 1537 1540 1589 -hsync +vsync
  gamma 1.0
EndSection

Section "screen"
  identifier "rechts"
  device "mill-onder"
  defaultdepth 24
  monitor "agneovo"
  SubSection "Display"
    depth 24
    modes "1600x1200@60" "1792x1344@60" "1400x1050@60" "1856x1392@60" "1280x1024@60" "1920x1440@60" "1280x960@60" "2048x1536@60" "1280x854" "1152x768@54" "1024x768@60" "800x600@60" "800x600@56" "640x480@60"
  EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
  Identifier "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "links" 0 0
  screen 1 "rechts" rightof "links"
  InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
  InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
  option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
  Mode 0666
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
  option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

```

### Post by 42Wired on 2007-03-21
> **wankel said:**
> After some fiddling with xorg.conf, I got a working set for my setup. I post it here mainly for two reasons:
- helping others with a similar setup
- possibly have someone shed a light on an issue that remains.

1. The peculiar thing about my setup is that is has somewhat older cards: two Matrox Millenium 2MB cards, with an extra 2MB addon card making 4 meg per card.
My problem was that my computer would freeze on starting X after booting my pc normally. On configuring, there would be no problem.
I have found the solution in booting in recovery mode (that is, just making sure X does not get started). Then I run "X -configure", just for the fun of my second card getting initiated.
After that I can enter runlevel 5 (telinit 5) without a problem.

2. I am running two monitors at 1600*1200, but I would like to turn them to portrait, as many of you in this tread are trying as well. 
I understand from the conversation so far, that "Option" "Rotate" 270 is not an option as long as Xinerama is "true" as well. 
Does anyone of you have experience with a trick or card that will do this? I am planning to upgrade my graphic cards to something with DVI on it, being two PCI cards or one AGP with dual DVI. Any succestions?

I thought there would be some way to use the Xrandr command through the command console, but I tried all I could think of (but I really was just guessing). Are you familiar with it?

### Post by Laen on 2007-03-21
Thanks for the time and effort writing this howto. This has been the only reason that's been keeping me from going back to Linux... And it still is.
I spent my afternoon trying out different versions of the xorg setup, but they were all failing miserably.

Also using a Intel 945 GM on a laptop with an external LCD. I tried using "CRT,LFP", "LFP, LFP2"... Sometimes they both worked to some extend, sometimes only one did. Best I could do is get my external monitor to run on the same resolution as the laptop (1280x800), sometimes it worked at 640x480. I guess for some reason the x server couldn't make it run at its native resolution (1680x1050). I don't have the monitor's actual display size from the manual, just its frequency rates, but that shouldn't be a problem.

Interestingly, if I turn off the laptop screen using the function key before entering X and then use an xorg config that only contains the external monitor, upon startx I get a message from the monitor saying "Out of Range 90.0 kHz / 60 Hz", although the xorg.conf clearly states that a horizontal frequency of 30-83 has to be used.

Either way, after much frustration I gave up. Hopefully someday features like this would be more straight-forward in Linux so that we won't have to reserve whole days just to get something working, which is supposed to work on the fly anyway.
I saw so many topics in different forums with people trying to get their dual monitor setups working and running into troubles that remain unresolved, it's just sad!

---

### Post by mully on 2007-03-23
Has anybody had any luck getting dual monitors to work with a Trident driver?  That is what I have in my Toshiba A25-207 laptop.  

When I add a second "Screen" to my "ServerLayout" section, I get a "Requested Entity Already in Use!" error.  

If I comment out the second "Screen" attribute, I am able to get the same video to display at the same time on both my internal Toshiba monitor and my external LCD monitor.  (I think this is called "Clone Mode".)  

I am unable to get MultiHead or a combined extended desktop to work.  

Here is the relevant xorg.conf file that causes the "Requested Entity Already in Use!" error.  

Thanks!

```

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Trident"
	Driver		"trident"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          0
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 Trident"
	Driver		"trident"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          1
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Laptop Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Secondary Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"0 Trident"
	Monitor		"Laptop Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Secondary Screen"
	Device		"1 Trident"
	Monitor		"Secondary Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
        Screen	      0	"Default Screen"
        Screen	      1	"Secondary Screen" RightOf "Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
        Option "Xinerama" "on"
        Option "Clone" "off"
EndSection

```

### Post by veloce on 2007-03-25
> **mully said:**
> 

```

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Trident"
	Driver		"trident"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          0
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"
EndSection


```

I'm not sure if it will fix your issues, but you shouldn't have a space after the comma in the following line:

        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"

I'm told that the space is interpreted as a null for the second monitor. Fix it in both device sections see if it helps.

---

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	#Load 	"GLcore"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dbe"
	#Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"it"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier "LCD"
	Driver "i810"
	Option "MonitorLayout"	"CRT,LFP"
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Screen 0
	BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier "CRT"
	Driver "i810"
	Option "MonitorLayout"	"CRT,LFP"
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Screen 1
	BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30 - 81
	VertRefresh	56 - 76
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"CRT"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30 - 81
	VertRefresh	56 - 76
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"LCD"
	Device		"LCD"
	Monitor		"LCD"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"CRT"
	Device		"CRT"
	Monitor		"CRT"
	DefaultDepth	24

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes	"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"LCDandCRT"
	Screen		0 "LCD"
	Screen		1 "CRT" RightOf "LCD"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option		"Xinerama" "true"
#	Option 		"AIGLX" "true"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"DefaultServerLayout" "LCDandCRT"
EndSection

#Section "DRI"
#	Mode	0666
#EndSection

#Section "Extensions"
#	Option		"Composite" "Enable"
#EndSection

### Post by veloce on 2007-03-26
With Intel graphics cards you often need to install "915resolution" to get all the resolutions supported by your hardware.

---

Basically I can't drag applications from one screen to the other, and if I try to start a second firefox in the second screen I get an error saying that an instance of firefox is already running.  My desktop icons however only appear on the main screen which makes me think that xinerama is on in some capacity.

I'm hoping that I just missed something small and that I can fix this pretty quickly.

Here's my xorg.conf file.  I cleaned it up a bit by removing the comment header, and the input device sections (which for some obscure reason include a bunch of wacom tablet junk)

note: If you read my xorg.conf file below you'll probably notice that I'm using two heads on the same card and that I should be using twinview.  I have gotten twinview to work, but I have a quad head matrox card that I'd like to use also (6 total monitors) and I'm pretty sure this will require xinerama rather than twinview.  At the moment I'm using the 2 monitors on the Geforce 6600 as a test case before messing with all the extra screens and the matrox card.

xorg.conf (prettified)
[HTML]Section "ServerFlags"
    Option 	   "Xinerma" "true"
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen	0  "Default Screen"
    Screen	1  "Screen 1" RightOf "Default Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    Option 	   "Xinerma" "On"
EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
    FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
    FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"

### Post by veloce on 2007-04-05
> **tutomlin said:**
> Hi, 
So far this has worked pretty well for me and I have two screen up and viewable, however the screens act like xinerama is not running.  Basically I can't drag applications from one screen to the other, and if I try to start a second firefox in the second screen I get an error saying that an instance of firefox is already running.  My desktop icons however only appear on the main screen which makes me think that xinerama is on in some capacity.


```
Section "ServerFlags"
    Option 	   "Xinerma" "true"
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen	0  "Default Screen"
    Screen	1  "Screen 1" RightOf "Default Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
    InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
    InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
    Option 	   "Xinerma" "On"
EndSection

```



1. Xinerama should have two "a"s.

2. I had trouble after getting xinerama going that gnome had "remembered" some stuff about the desktop and was getting confused.  I couldn't drag windows into certain areas of the screen.  The fix was to delete the directory:
~/.gconf/desktop/gnome/screen (where ~ is your home directory.)

3. Xinerama creates one notional desktop across your monitors - starting firefox in the second screen is the same as starting in the first.

### Post by veloce on 2007-04-08
That xorg.conf looks alright to me.  Two things to try:

1. set your default depth to 16 for both monitors.  I have issues with screen memory over the two monitors.

2. Sometimes gnome remembers layout stuff that screws up the screen - you can't drag windows into certain parts of the screen and so forth.  The solution I found on the forums was to delete the following directory: 
[home directory]/.gconf/desktop/gnome/screen

I grasping at straws here though.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#

# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

### Post by nico08 on 2007-04-10

The laptop monitor stays black all the time. I reverted to my backup config file, but that didn't solve the problem. I have a dual boot, and Windows no longer recognizes the laptop screen either. Any ideas what could be causing this?

On your own case, with only one of the two screens to be rotated: I have been investigating in that direction since my parents' setup also involves one of the screens being "portraited" to get most of the screen estate. ### Post by nico08 on 2007-04-16
> **wankel said:**
> Could it be that the BIOS got mixed up? The fact that your Dell splashscreen goes missing as well suggests it is not something to fix from within an OS.

I looked in the BIOS setup menu and didn't see anything related to the monitor. This has happened a couple of times now. The first time, I didn't think that the problem was related to my changes and assumed that it was a hardware issue (since it affected both OSs and the BIOS). I had the monitor replaced, which fixed the problem until several days later, when I tried to set up dual monitors again and the exact same problem happened.

My only guesses are that the BIOS or video card got misconfigured during the process, or the monitor is actually broken (this is hard to test because the monitor has a nonstandard connection, so I'd have to find another laptop to take apart in order to check the monitor). The latter seems unlikely, but if it is the case, people should be aware that this procedure can potentially ruin their monitor.

### Post by mully on 2007-04-17
> **veloce said:**
> I'm not sure if it will fix your issues, but you shouldn't have a space after the comma in the following line:

        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"

I'm told that the space is interpreted as a null for the second monitor. Fix it in both device sections see if it helps.
Veloce, a belated thanks for replying to my post.  (I haven't been back to the forums in a few weeks.) 

I will try your suggestion this evening when I am infront of my Ubuntu box and let you know.  I hope it is really that simple!

UPDATE:  I tried removing the space after the comma with the same "Requested entity already in use" error.  I guess my Toshiba Trident driver does not support multiple displays with Ubuntu.  What is strange about that is that extended desktop works fine with Windows XP on my Toshiba.  I'm not sure why my video driver would support the functionality on 1 OS but not the other...

Thanks again!

An extra big thanks to Ziox for writing the how-to (nice work champ!) and veloce, whose revelation of deleting the ~/.gconf/desktop/gnome/screen directory was the last puzzle piece!

The code below outputs 1024x768 (which should be the output to the Laptop screen) to the external monitor, and if I change "MonitorLayout" to "CRT,LFP", I get 1024x768 on the laptop, but nothing on the external monitor. If I change it to "CRT,CRT+LFP", I get an output on both monitors that's a clone ( 1024x768 ), so I'm not really sure what's going on. Seems to me that PipeA just doesn't work. I'm using an HP nc4200 with a local 12" flat panel and a VGA port (Intel 915 I think). Thanks for any help! 

EDIT: A grep for Pipe on /var/log/Xorg.0.log gives:
```
(II) I810(0): No active displays on Pipe A.
(II) I810(0): Currently active displays on Pipe B:
(==) I810(0): Display is using Pipe B
(II) I810(0): Display plane A is disabled and connected to Pipe A.
(II) I810(0): Display plane B is enabled and connected to Pipe B.
(II) I810(0): Display plane A is now disabled and connected to Pipe A.
(II) I810(0): Display plane B is now enabled and connected to Pipe B.
```

If I try
```
Option   "FixedPipe"   "A"
```
the log simply gives
```
No active displays on Pipe A
```
and then reverts to B.

EDIT: After some uming and ahhing, I've got it. I've left the original xorg.conf with the original problems directly below this text, and the working xorg directly below that. A summary of the differences is available at the bottom of the post, in case anybody runs across similar problems.

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel0"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Option		"MonitorLayout"		"LFP,CRT"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel1"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Option		"MonitorLayout"		"LFP,CRT"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"LaptopScreen"
	Option		"DPMS"
	Option		"DDCMode"	"True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier 	"ExternalDisplay"
	Option 		"DPMS"
	Option		"DDCMode"	"True"
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen0"
	Device		"Intel0"
	Monitor		"LaptopScreen"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier 	"Screen1"
	Device 		"Intel1"
	Monitor 	"ExternalDisplay"
	DefaultDepth 	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth 		24
		Modes 		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier 	"Multihead"
	Screen 		0	"Screen0"
	Screen 		1	"Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
	Option 		"Xinerama"	 "true"
	InputDevice 	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice 	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel0"

	Option		"MonitorLayout"		"CRT,LFP"

	InputDevice 	"Generic Keyboard"

EndSection

	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by FuriousLettuce on 2007-04-21
I use a laptop which I connect to an external display at home. I wanted to make it easy to turn xinerama on and off by swapping xorg files, so I've written a small script which automates the process. I have two xorg files: xorg.conf.bak, which is my original, single screen config, and xorg.conf.xine, which is my xinerama dual screen config. It's not a terribly pretty solution, and I'm sure somebody else could come up with a better one, but it does the job.

As I mentioned, this is intended for people who have to swap between single and multiple monitors often. 

Initially you want two xorg files - one for a single monitor (as I mentioned, I called it xorg.conf.bak), and one for xinerama (mine's xorg.conf.xine). I'd also make a backup of both somewhere (your home folder perhaps), just to make sure you can revert if something goes wrong.

Open a terminal and do:
```
nano .switchXine.sh
```
then type / paste in the following code:
```
#!/bin/bash

if [ "$(cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf | grep -ci xinerama)" != 0 ]; then
        cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf.bak /etc/X11/xorg.conf
else
        cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf.xine /etc/X11/xorg.conf
fi
```
Remember to swap username for your username, and change the names of the xorg files if you have them stored somewhere else. The script checks whether or not xinerama (case insensitive) appears in xorg.conf (e.g., for this to work, the single screen xorg.conf must have no mention of xinerama in it) and then swaps the xinerama and normal config files in and out accordingly.

We have to make it executable:
```
chmod +x .switchXine.sh
```

To test it, do 
```
sudo ./.switchXine.sh
```
then just do
```
less /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
to see if it's changed. If it has, we want to put it back to normal for the moment, so simply redo
```
sudo ./.switchXine.sh
```.

Next, we want to make it so that we can run it without having to enter our username, so do
```
sudo visudo
```
and then enter the following on a line at the bottom
```
username     ALL=NOPASSWD:   /home/username/.switchXine.sh
```
remembering to swap both 'username' references to your own username. Save the file. Now we can run it without having to enter the password!

The final thing to make it easy to use is to turn it into a panel launcher, so right-click on your panel and choose 'Add to Panel', then choose 'Custom Application Launcher'. Change the type in the drop-down box to 'Application in Terminal', name it, then in the command box, type
```
sudo /home/username/.switchXine.sh
```
where username is your username. The comment can be anything - it' the text that appears when you hover over the button. Finally, we can make it look pretty. If you click the 'Icon' button in the top left, you can give it a nice icon to fit with your desktop. I chose 'gnome-windows.png' in /usr/share/pixmaps because it is a picture of two windows that look vaguely like monitors - see top of attached pic. Now you can simply click this button, press Ctrl+Alt+Backspace to restart X, and voila - you're now switching between one and multiple monitors in one click and one key combination!

If anybody has any questions or anything, just shout me back. I hope this is useful to at least somebody!

NB: As long as you've properly backed up your files, I can't see there being a problem with this idea, but I can't be held responsible for any damages that occur as a result of attempting to execute this idea.

---

        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen          "Default Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection

```

### Post by veloce on 2007-04-23
What version of Ubuntu are you running? Feisty has some significant improvements in this area.

Some things to start with:

> **StreLochKi said:**
> 

my conf
```
Section "Device"
        Identifier      "0 Intel Corporation 82945G/GZ Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:1"
        Screen          0
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "DFP,LPF"
EndSection

```


I think that should be LFP not LPF?

> **StreLochKi said:**
> 
```

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 Intel Corporation 82945G/GZ Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
        Screen          1
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "DPF,LFP"
EndSection

```


Where did you get the BusIDs?  This may be the tricky bit with your setup.  On my laptop, to run two screens (internal and external) they both use the same BusID. (Even though two IDs are reportedly there.)

Where is your ServerLayout section?

### Post by FuriousLettuce on 2007-04-24
> **StreLochKi said:**
> Hello,

I want a dual screen desktop  

I have a strange HW configuration,
I have a HP compaq dc7600 with an on-board graphics card
in my PCI EX slot is a expansion of that graphics card
this means 1 driver
it works in windows
here is what I have

output
```

Screen Main  Screen Doesn't exist: deleting placement
I810: No Matching device section for instance (BusID PCI :0:2:0)
Screen 0 Deleted because of no matching config section
Divice(s) detected but none match those in the config file

Fatal server Error
No screens found

```

tnx

You don't want to be changing the bus ID's - 0:2:0 is the right one (as confirmed by the single busID reported by lspci). One thing to check is, are you outputting through a DVI cable or a VGA cable? If it's a VGA cable, you want to change that DFP to CRT - CRT is just the placeholder for a VGA connection, not necessarily a CRT. DFP always implies you are using a digital connection. 

The output that I've quoted has a double space between 'Main' and 'Screen' on the first line, but I can't find the corresponding entry in your dual screen conf. There is however a mistake below the "Section 'Screen'" for the second screen - the EndSection that ought to be at the bottom of the section is in fact right at the bottom of the conf, which will mess things up, well worth moving that back up to where it ought to be (at the end of the relevant screen section).

Section "Screen 0"

Section "Screen 1"

Section "ServerLayout"

	Identifier	"Dualhead - xinerama"

[/Code]



1. The sections should be "Monitor" and "Screen" not "Monitor 0" etc. 

2. Your ServerLayout section having an identifier other than default means that you need to specify it when you start xorg, are you doing that?

3. Feisty has some additional features for dual monitors that don't require xinerama.  I now don't use it - I end up with two screens each with their own session, I can move the mouse between them but not drag windows from one to the other. Depending how you want to use your system, you may find this more useful.

### Post by newblacknoise on 2007-04-30
Luca Dod3,

I experienced similar problems when I first tried setting up xinerama, but I eventually got it working. Do not despair!

It would help if you were more specific about your setup (are you using two external monitors, or a laptop with one external monitor? etc.), and post your modified xorg.conf file.

The cause of my problems was partly just getting my xorg.conf file just right (it took a few attempts, but I got there), and also setting my proper screen resolution. I have intel onboard graphics on my laptop, and I needed to use a handy utility called 915resolution to setup the screen resolution for my external monitor.

good luck!

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

# package.
#

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"

EndSection

	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"it"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier      "Intel-LCD"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		0
        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Intel-VGA"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
        Screen          1
        Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT+LFP"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"LCD"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "LCD2"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       31-81
        VertRefresh     50-76
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "LCD-Screen"
        Device          "Intel-LCD"
        Monitor         "LCD"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "CRT-Screen"
        Device          "Intel-VGA"
        Monitor         "LCD2"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier      "Multihead"
        Screen          0 "LCD-Screen"
        Screen          1 "CRT-Screen" RightOf "LCD-Screen"
        Option "Xinerama" "on"
        Option "Clone" "off"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-01
That second one looks good except for the "monitorlayout" lines.  These would be better like:

       Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"

Note: DON'T have a space after the comma.  Apparently the space is interpreted as a NULL monitor and the rest of the line is ignored.

Also, if you're using feisty, be sure to try the same file but with xinerama off: you get two nearly independent sessions on the two monitors - I find that more useful than on session spread over two monitors.

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-02
I'm running out of ideas here, sorry.  You could try finding the monitor specs for the other monitor (refresh and sync).

Here's my xorg.conf for comparison.  I have a my laptop and an external lcd monitor. 

```
##########################################################################################
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This version by: Kerry Mayes, February 2007.
# For Dell Latitude D520 with external Viewsonic VE710b Monitor connected via docking station.
# 
# As an edited file, it will not be automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades. 
#
# I have not run the following command to allow it to be automatically updated:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#
# Updated by: Kerry Mayes, May 2007 for Feisty changes
# Xinerama no longer used as xorg now runs a session for each monitor.
##########################################################################################
#
# These sections have not been altered except to update for Feisty
Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# First device section, will control the Laptop LCD screen
Section "Device"
       Identifier      "D520 945GM 0"
       Driver "i810"
       BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
       # This next line is required for xinerama.
       Screen 0
       # It was stated somewhere that the LFP is always the second pipe. Make sure never 
       # to have a space after the "," it's interpreted as "None". 
       Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
       # I don't think this next line is actually required.
		 Option "DevicePresence" "on"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# Second device section, will control the external screen 
Section "Device"
       Identifier "D520 945GM 1"
       Driver "i810"
		 # Even though there is a second pci address for the card, only this one is used for 
		 # the external monitor.  The other may be for the svideo out but that has not been
		 # confirmed.
       BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
       # This next line is required for xinerama.
       Screen 1
       # It was stated somewhere that the LFP is always the second pipe. Make sure never 
       # to have a space after the "," it's interpreted as "None". 
       Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
       # I don't think this next line is actually required.
		 Option "DevicePresence" "on"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# First Monitor section, for the Laptop LCD screen.
Section "Monitor"
   Identifier      "D520 Monitor"
   # I can't find a definitive source for these values, but these are the ones Fiesty came up with.
	HorizSync	28-70
	VertRefresh	43-60
   DisplaySize     304 228     # From manual
   Option          "DPMS"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# Second Monitor section, for the Viewsonic monitor connected to the docking station.
Section "Monitor"
       Identifier "Viewsonic VE710b Monitor"
       # These values are from the monitor's manual.
       HorizSync       30-82
       VertRefresh     50-75
       DisplaySize	338 270
       Option "DPMS"
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# First screen section, connects the first device to the Laptop LCD screen.
# Note that the resolution "1400x1050" wasn't available until I had installed 915resolution
Section "Screen"
       Identifier      "D520 Screen"
       Device          "D520 945GM 0"
       Monitor         "D520 Monitor"
       DefaultDepth    24
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth           24
               Modes           "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" 
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth           16
               Modes           "1400x1050" "1280x1024" "1024x768" 
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# Second screen section, connects the second device to the Viewsonic monitor connected to 
# the docking station.
Section "Screen"
       Identifier "Viewsonic VE710b Screen"
       Device "D520 945GM 1"
       Monitor "Viewsonic VE710b Monitor"
       DefaultDepth 24
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth 24
               Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768"
               # This next line has been found useful when xinerama automatically makes 
               # the two screens the same virtual size - this command can be used on the 
               # lower resolution screen to limit it to the resolution it can display 
               # without scrolling.
               # virtual 1280 1024
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
       SubSection "Display"
               Depth 16
               Modes "1280x1024" "1024x768"
               # This next line has been found useful when xinerama automatically makes 
               # the two screens the same virtual size - this command can be used on the 
               # lower resolution screen to limit it to the resolution it can display 
               # without scrolling.
               # virtual 1280 1024
               viewport 0 0
       EndSubSection
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################
#
# The serverlayout section to put it all together.  
# Currently I use this file for both Laptop only and Dual screens so have to comment things 
# out or put them back in each time I switch.
#
Section "ServerLayout"
       Identifier      "Default Layout"
       # For the Intel, having a screen number in these lines seems to get it confused.
       Screen       "D520 Screen" 0 0
       # Comment out the next two lines to use just the laptop screen.        
       Screen       "Viewsonic VE710b Screen" LeftOf "D520 Screen"
#       Option "Xinerama" "on"
       Option "Clone" "off"
       InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
       InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
       InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
#
# Sometimes gnome remembers layout stuff that screws up the screen - you can't drag windows
# into certain parts of the screen and so forth.  The solution I found on the forums was to
# delete the following directory: [home directory]/.gconf/desktop/gnome/screen
#
#
##########################################################################################
#
# These sections not altered.
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
       Mode    0666
EndSection
#
##########################################################################################

```

xorg.conf

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	#Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
	Identifier	"Main Device"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	#Option		"DisplayInfo" "FALSE"
	Screen		0
	#Option		"VBERestore" "false"
	#Option		"DDC" "true"
	#Option		"Monitor Layout" "LFP, LFP2"
	#Option		"Monitor Layout" "CRT, LFP2"
	Option		"Monitor Layout" "CRT, LFP"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	#Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82852/855GM Integrated Graphics Device"
	Identifier	"External Device"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:1"
	#Option		"DisplayInfo" "FALSE"
	Screen		1
	#Option		"VBERestore" "false"
	#Option		"DDC" "true"
	#Option		"Monitor Layout" "LFP, LFP2"
	#Option		"Monitor Layout" "CRT, LFP2"
	#Option		"Monitor Layout" "CRT, LFP"
	
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
	#Option		"Monitor Layout" "LFP, LFP2"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"External Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Laptop Screen"
	Device		"Main Device"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"External Screen"
	Device		"External Device"
	Monitor		"External Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Laptop Screen"
	Screen		"External Screen" RightOf "Laptop Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Option		"Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

#Section "ServerFlags"
#	Option		"Xinerama"
#EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection


```

The BIOS sets the PCI (sis card) as primary, so while the pc is starting up it runs on the PCI (sis, Right Monitor) but as soon as the graphic mode comes (login window) it runs only on the AGP (nVidia, Left Monitor).

What can i do?

	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option	    "AIGLX" "off"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "stylus"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "eraser"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "eraser"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
	Option	    "Type" "cursor"
	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies Inc ATI Radeon XPRESS 200M 5955 (PCIE)"
	Driver      "ati"
	BusID       "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	Option	    "DesktopSetup" "clone"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Default Screen"
	Device     "ATI Technologies Inc ATI Radeon XPRESS 200M 5955 (PCIE)"
	Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     1
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     4
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     8
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     15
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     16
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option	    "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection
 Section "ServerFlags"   
 Option "BlankTime" "0"    
Option "StandbyTime" "0"   
 Option "SuspendTime" "0"   
 Option "OffTime" "0" 
EndSection
```


If you have any tips that would be great!

I have three options.

1. Some one helps me to get this to work in ubuntu
2. Some one helps me to do this using Windows in Innotek Virtual Box.
3. I install a windows partition in my laptop and use windows when I need to do a presentation.
I don't believe 2 will work at all but who knows.

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-10
> **Luca Dod3 said:**
> I managed to almost get it to work (Intel card, laptop 1024x768, external 1440x900), but it doesn't seem to use well the resolution of the external monitor! it makes it smaller and longer, so that to see everything I've got to move the mouse to the border.
I've installed the 915 resolutions but nothing. I'm using xinerama. Maybe putting the screen size could help?


I can't quite follow what your symptoms are.  Are you getting both screens working?  

Some things to try:
1. Some resolution issues with dual monitors can be caused by lowered screen **depth**. Try adding a 16 bit resolution to both monitors.

2. If you are using feisty, try turning off xinerama - you should get two screens working as separate sessions.

---

### Post by ignignokt00 on 2007-05-10
Xinerama worked great for me with my ATI card, but only if I log into a Gnome session.  Is there any way I can get it to work in an Xgl/Beryl session?
When I try to log into Xgl, I just get the screens to show the login screen orange color for about 10 seconds, then X restarts (without a notification that the session terminated early).  Any suggestions?

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-05-10
Xinerama disables Direct Rendering, which is one of the requirements for XGL.  Basically Xinerama cannot be used with XGL or AIGLX.  I would suggest you to try MergedFB or Big-Desktop.

---

### Post by ignignokt00 on 2007-05-11
Thanks.

---

	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen	0	"Main Screen"
	Screen	1	"Second Screen"	RightOf	"Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option		"Twinview" "true"
EndSection


Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

p.p.s.: sorry for the bad english;

First thing to try is to see if gnome has got itself confused.  It tries to remember settings and can get things rather messed up.  Solution is to delete the following folder:

From your home folder:
```
sudo rm -r ./.gconf/desktop/gnome/screen
```

Second thing to try is to add a depth 16 set of resolutions: sometime xorg drops down to 16 if it can't handle 24.

---

You should check out the nVidia Twinview guide.  It is rather useless to use Xinerama if you have a nvidia card.

> **johnnyphive said:**

As Ziox said, you should be using twinview.  There is an option for the i810 to "FlipPrimary" to do what you want - you'll have to find the equivalent command for the nvidia driver.

> **Luca Dod3 said:**
> You can't imagine how much I love you!!!!
it is finally working! just to little things missing.

I could use it with xinerama off, I like the idea of two separated monitors, BUT I can't find the way to move a window from a monitor to the other. is that possible? (always with xinerama off).

anyway thanks for everything you've done till now. I'll never stop thanking you.
Luca Dod3.

Unfortunately, with xinerama off windows won't move from one screen to another.  The only thing that moves across is the mouse.   

On the damaged, have a search of this forum.  I've seen mention of similar problems but haven't followed them through to conclusion as I haven't experienced them.

(And you are most welcome. I've had a lot of help getting my system working, so am pleased to be able to pass on what I've learnt.)

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-19
knaka, these lines:

Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, CRT"

should not have a space after the comma.  xorg interprets the space as a null monitor and ignores the rest of the line.

If you are using Feisty, try it without the xinerama option.  The latest xorg in Feisty will run two seperate sessions on two monitors without xinerama.  (Xinerama is needed to make them into a single desktop).

All that said, isn't there a better alternative to xinerama if you have ati hardware?

---

Any other tips?

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-19
> **nerdman978 said:**
> Nothing happens at all for me! The X server doesn't crash (surprising as this usually happens) and Xinerama doesn't work!

Here is xorg.conf

So it's not nothing.  I assume that you just get one monitor working.  This will be because you have two server layout sections and xorg is picking the first one (with just one monitor).

---

### Post by knaka on 2007-05-20
> **veloce said:**
> Some questions:
Is "DDCMode" valid for the via driver?  I can only find reference to it in the "ati" driver.

You have the same horizsync and vertrefresh ranges for both monitors, is this correct?

With both screens plugged in, what happens when you start X?

I have now removed "DDCMode" but that does not make any difference,and i also changed the resolution for both screens down to 1024x768 and i removed "horisync and "vertrefresh". But still i have the same problem.

If i run my working xorg file with only one monitor configured then when I reboot my computer with my "CRT" attached it works fine under 1024x768, but i dont get any picture on my laptop only on my external screen. And if I reboot and disconnect the external screen my laptop screen is working fine, and this is with the exact same xorg file.

When I start X with both screens attached and the new xorg file i get this message:
Fatal Server  Error:
Requested Entity already in use!

any more tips?

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-20
> **knaka said:**
> I have now removed "DDCMode" but that does not make any difference,and i also changed the resolution for both screens down to 1024x768 and i removed "horisync and "vertrefresh". But still i have the same problem.

If i run my working xorg file with only one monitor configured then when I reboot my computer with my "CRT" attached it works fine under 1024x768, but i dont get any picture on my laptop only on my external screen. And if I reboot and disconnect the external screen my laptop screen is working fine, and this is with the exact same xorg file.

When I start X with both screens attached and the new xorg file i get this message:
Fatal Server  Error:
Requested Entity already in use!

any more tips?

I think there's something hardware related going on there to switch the default screen from internal to external.  

The "requested entity in use" could mean that your config file accidentally has the same identifier for the two screens/monitors/devices.  But I've looked at the last copy you posted and it didn't seem to be the case.

The other thing I wonder is whether, for your hardware, you should have different Bus IDs for the two devices.  For my intel hardware, I thought that I needed to do that and it definitely wasn't the case (despite my Device Manager showing two pci graphic controllers).  You could try that.

I've also just been looking at the man page for the via driver ([http://www.die.net/doc/linux/man/man4/via.4.html](http://www.die.net/doc/linux/man/man4/via.4.html)) and see that quite a bit of your xorg.conf file is i810 commands (which is what I'm using, so didn't notice before).  The monitor layout option will be being ignored as it doesn't apply to via. You need to use the "active device" option - and I'm not sure how you format that for multiple devices.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-21
> **mpanahi said:**
> 

Hello, trying to get a dual monitor setup working with my laptop. All I get now is a black screen on my external crt, and my laptop screen becomes either white, or has bright colored vertical lines. Not sure what to do. Pretty sure the modeline and other info is correct for crt -- googled it and also found it listed in the log file.

My conf file is below and my log file is posted [here]("http://link.ece.uci.edu/~mpanahi/Xorg.0.log.txt"). 

Thanks in advance for your help.

1. I assume you have 915resolution installed, yes?

2. Your monitor layout lines should have LFp as the second pipe. from my xorg.conf:
       # It was stated somewhere that the LFP is always the second pipe. Make sure never 
       # to have a space after the "," it's interpreted as "None". 
       Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"

3. The line "option ddcmode" seems to be unable to load for at least one of your monitors.  Are you sure this line is valid for the i810 driver?  Try commenting it out.

4. If you are using Feisty, comment out the Xinerama line until you get the two monitors displaying correctly - then add it back in if you want to do the single desktop thing.

---
