### Post by veloce on 2007-05-22
Your log file seems to show that xorg has difficulty with horizontal sync on both screens.  It seems to eliminate all the normal resolutions, so it is trying to use resolutions that don't work.  Try setting the horizontal sync and vertical refresh for the laptop - can you find it in a manual?  And check the line for the crt again.  (I know you said you got these details correct, just check that the line is formatted correctly).

Beyond that, I've run out of ideas.

---

   I'm trying to get multhead working on a Dell Latitude D820 with a LG Flattron L1952 display connected to the external port.
The Display on the Laptop still comes up so it is usable, but nothing displays on the LG display.
Any Ideas?

Thanks;

I'm running ubuntu 7.04
klandrus@klandrus-laptop:~$ uname -a
Linux klandrus-laptop 2.6.20-15-generic #2 SMP Sun Apr 15 06:17:24 UTC 2007 x86_64 GNU/Linux


here's the output from lspci;
00:02.0 VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 03)
00:02.1 Display controller: Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 03)

Here's the xorg.conf ;
```
Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "cursor"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"         # Tablet PC ONLY

Section "Monitor"

        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       28-96
        VertRefresh     43-60

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Second Monitor"

        VertRefresh     43-60

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "0 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"

                Depth           1

        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           16
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default"
        Screen          "Default Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Multihead"
        Screen          "Default Screen"
        Screen          "Second Screen" RightOf "Default Screen"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option "xinerama" "true"
        Option "DefaultServerLayout" "Multihead"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-22
> **klandrus said:**
> Hello;
   I'm trying to get multhead working on a Dell Latitude D820 with a LG Flattron L1952 display connected to the external port.
The Display on the Laptop still comes up so it is usable, but nothing displays on the LG display.
Any Ideas?

Thanks;

I'm running ubuntu 7.04
klandrus@klandrus-laptop:~$ uname -a
Linux klandrus-laptop 2.6.20-15-generic #2 SMP Sun Apr 15 06:17:24 UTC 2007 x86_64 GNU/Linux


here's the output from lspci;
00:02.0 VGA compatible controller: Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 03)
00:02.1 Display controller: Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller (rev 03)


1. both devices use the same pci bus 00:02:00 (I know it seems counter intuitive but it is right).
2. You need an "option" "monitorlayout" "CRT,LFP" line in each device section.

---

# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7800 GT]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:4:0:0"
	Screen      0
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7800 GT]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:4:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"2 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7800 GT]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
	Screen      2
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Apple Cinema"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-96
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"'
	HorizSync	31-80
	VertRefresh	60-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Third Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	31-80
	VertRefresh	60-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"0 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7800 GT]"
	Monitor		"Apple Cinema"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1920x1200"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Second Screen"
    Device        "1 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7800 GT]"
    Monitor        "Second Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1280x1024"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Third Screen"
    Device        "2 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7800 GT]"
    Monitor        "Third Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1280x1024"
    EndSubSection
EndSection



Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Default Screen"
	Screen		1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Default Scren"
	Screen		2 "Third Screen" RightOf "Second Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-23
> **plunderisley said:**
> just wont work right
2 video cards, 3 monitors. Wont start up X


My suggestions:
[LIST=1]
[*]Walk before you run - set up two monitors first, then add the third.
[*]Use nvidea's twinview
[/LIST]

---

### Post by plunderisley on 2007-05-23
> **veloce said:**
> My suggestions:
[LIST=1]
[*]Walk before you run - set up two monitors first, then add the third.
[*]Use nvidea's twinview
[/LIST]

I got all 3 working with the Xinerama. But when that is enabled, I cant use my screensavers, terminal, etc. With it off, the monitors still work and so does terminal, but just no monitor dragging.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-24
> **plunderisley said:**
> I got all 3 working with the Xinerama. But when that is enabled, I cant use my screensavers, terminal, etc. With it off, the monitors still work and so does terminal, but just no monitor dragging.

Not quite sure of your problem under Xinerama - can you describe it in a bit more detail?

Without Xinerama, sounds like standard behaviour under Feisty.  Three relatively independent sessions.  That's how I use my two monitors.  Beware of a bug in the screensaver that will start it up if there is no activity in the main screen - doesn't matter how much activity there is in the subordinate ones, it just operates on the main one!

---

### Post by plunderisley on 2007-05-24
When I have Xinerama enabled, I cannot open up Terminal in gnome. I cannot use the screensavers. The GUI crashes randomly and gives me the login window.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-30
eball:

The following lines should not have a space after the comma. xorg interprets that as a null monitor.

Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT"

I'm not sure if this is all of your problem as the first screen should still work.

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-05-30
The "monitorlayout" option is only for multiple screens connected to the same card.  Your first two devices should only have two items and your third device shouldn't have this line at all.

Other than that, it all looks good.  (Though I don't know if you're using the right driver either. You must be able to google an answer to that question.)

---

I've got dual Dell 2005FPW's and they are acting as a single display (the good part). The problem I'm having is that only the primary (DVI Input) flat panel is displaying at 1600x1050. The second (VGA Input) flat panel is displaying at some lower resolution (probably 1280 or something).  When I attempt to change/check the display resolution using System-->Preferences-->Screen Resolution, I get the following error: "The X Server does not support the XRandR extension. Runtime resolution changes to the display size are not available."

I can live with the dual-display configuration, but I would really like to get my second flat panel to display at the same 1600x1050 resolution as the primary.

### Post by MingShun on 2007-06-01
> I got an error screen after I followed the steps posted here. I'm trying to have dual screens (laptop screen 13.3in + dell external 17in LCD)

I'm running ubuntu 7.04, my graphics card is intel 945GM. This is all on a laptop (sony C290)

I am definitely glad that I created the backup!  I used it. Here is what I have in the xorg.conf that crashed:

...

Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP, LFP"
...

Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP, LFP"
...



First of all, change the identifiers of your Screens.  They're not supposed to be **Default Screen** according to your ServerLayout Section.  Apparently, they're supposed to be called **Main Monitor** and **Second Monitor**.  In other words, you need to goto the serverlayout section to pick new names for your screens, then you can update them.

If this isn't enough,

First use some black magic, and try deleting that space after the comma.
Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,LFP"

Afterwards, try replacing one of the LFP's with CRT's.
My Dell configuration uses Monitor Layout  "CRT,LFP"

If that doesn't work, try flipping it around: "LFP,CRT"

But keep that space out.

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ati9250"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:8:06:0"
        Screen          1
        Option          "DDCMode"               "True"
        Option          "Mon*itorLayout" "DFP,DFP"
EndSection


```

I don't think there should be an asterix in the monitorlayout line.  Actually, I don't think you should have these lines at all.  They are used for two monitors connected to one card.

### Post by baconstrip on 2007-06-02
> **veloce said:**
> I don't think there should be an asterix in the monitorlayout line.  Actually, I don't think you should have these lines at all.  They are used for two monitors connected to one card.

### Post by cyndessa on 2007-06-02
> **MingShun said:**
> First of all, change the identifiers of your Screens.  They're not supposed to be **Default Screen** according to your ServerLayout Section.  Apparently, they're supposed to be called **Main Monitor** and **Second Monitor**.  In other words, you need to goto the serverlayout section to pick new names for your screens, then you can update them.

If this isn't enough,

First use some black magic, and try deleting that space after the comma.
Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,LFP"

Afterwards, try replacing one of the LFP's with CRT's.
My Dell configuration uses Monitor Layout  "CRT,LFP"

If that doesn't work, try flipping it around: "LFP,CRT"

But keep that space out.

Thanks for your help!  With your help plus removing the 1 and 0 behind the Screen lines in the ServerLayout section, I was able to get it working!

---

Anyone had any issues with that?

### Post by veloce on 2007-06-03
> **enortham said:**
> I've been trying to get my laptop to work with an external lcd without much luck. 


Took me about a week!

Try these changes:

> **enortham said:**
> 
```

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		0
	Option 		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option          "MonitorLayout" "LFP,DFP"
        #Option          "DRI" "false"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
        Screen          1
	Option          "DDCMode" "True"
        Option          "MonitorLayout" "LFP,DFP"
        #Option          "DRI" "true"
EndSection


```


change both monitor layout to "CRT,LFP"

The second pipe is always the laptop monitor; DFP should only be used for a digital output from the card (unless you *are* using a digital output)

> **enortham said:**
> 
```



Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"0 Screen"
	# Comment the next two lines out to get xserver to come up again
        Screen          "1 Screen" RightOf "0 Screen"
	Option   	"Xinerama" "True"
	Option		"Clone" "off"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

```


On the screen lines, put in the screen numbers, e.g.

Screen 0  "0 Screen"

Also, if you are using Feisty, try having everything the same but Xinerama off.  Feisty will run the two screens as two desktops.  You can then get everything working on the two independently, before turning them in to one big desktop.

### Post by enortham on 2007-06-04
Veloce,

I made the changes you suggested and it performs similar except instead of vertical lines it's some repeating garbled imaged on both monitors. If I comment out the Xinerama line both monitors come up ok but in 1440x900 and both with the same view.  Of course my laptop can't handle the resolution so it only displays the top left of the screen whereas the other monitor displays it properly.

Eric

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-06-04
> **enortham said:**
> Veloce,

I made the changes you suggested and it performs similar except instead of vertical lines it's some repeating garbled imaged on both monitors. If I comment out the Xinerama line both monitors come up ok but in 1440x900 and both with the same view.  Of course my laptop can't handle the resolution so it only displays the top left of the screen whereas the other monitor displays it properly.

I should mention that I installed the intel video drivers via apt-get to get 1440x900 on my external lcd. I don't remember the exact package name and I'm not in front of my linux box to check but I don't know if that could cause a problem or not.

Any other suggestions?

Thanks,

Eric

I cannot get dual monitors to work with the "intel" driver and have not found anyone who has succeeded.  I've gone back to the "i810" for that reason. (Seeing anything on two screens with "intel" is a major achievement!)

The only way I know of doing what you want is to go back to the i810, and install 915resolution to get higher res on external monitor.  I think you will need to edit the 915resolution file to get two "tricky" resolutions added to the list.  (I've seen someone describe doing that on this forum.) 

You may need to add a mode line for each monitor to select the resolution as well.

Conclusion:  the new intel driver is great for getting the right resolutions but kills the ability to do dual screen.

With my xorg.conf file below, my panel and window list at the top and bottom of the screen are just gray bars that wiggle a little. Also, I haven't checked which process it is, but when it's in that state, my laptop runs incredibly slowly and eventually freezes.

If I change "Second Screen" Above "Main Screen"  to say "Second Screen" Below "Main Screen", everything works fine, but then my monitors are in the wrong orientation.

If I change my laptop resolution from 1400x1050 to 1280x1024, to match my desktop monitor, everything is fine except that my laptop is blurry.

Removing the virtual size of my desktop has no effect.

### Post by enortham on 2007-06-05
Thanks Veloce. I uninstalled the xorg intel drivers and reinstalled the i810 drivers and now I have dual monitors with a shared desktop. I can't believe I spent a day trying to get the other drivers to work. I don't recall seeing anyone else mention problems with them and a dual display. Oh well now the only problem is my external lcd is displaying 640x480 when it should be at 1440x900. 

I ran 
```

sudo 915resolution 50 1440 900

```

and restarted the with ctrl+alt+bkspc and the external display didn't display anything. I then restarted the computer and both displays came up in 1280x800. I ran

```

sudo 915resolution 50 1440 900

```

one more time and then restarted xserver again and now I finally have 1280x800 on one screen and 1440x900 on the other. The refresh rate doesn't look so hot but I'll check that out tomorrow. 

Let me know if you have any hints on how to set the display and card to the optimal settings.

Cheers!

Eric

### Post by veloce on 2007-06-11
It may not be your only issue, but you need to remove the space after the comma in your monitorlayout lines.  (The space is interpreted as a null monitor).

---

> It may not be your only issue, but you need to remove the space after the comma in your monitorlayout lines.  (The space is interpreted as a null monitor).

Tried now, but didn't worked.

Thanks
Stefano

### Post by veloce on 2007-06-13
I don't have an ati based card so ymmv, but you shouldn't have a space after the comma in these lines:

Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS, TMDS"

The space is interpreted as a "null" monitor and the TMDS is ignored.

There may be more to it, but that's a start.

---

### Post by enortham on 2007-06-19
I by no means am an expert on this so anybody feel free to ring in if I'm off base. Drenesh, I think your MonitorLayout is wrong. I think the MonitorLayout should be identical for both your device sections since it really is the same physical device; that's at least my guess. The docs at [http://www.xfree86.org/current/i810.4.html](http://www.xfree86.org/current/i810.4.html) aren't that clear for MonitorLayout but I think CRT+LFP,DFP+DFP2 means pipe A runs the CRT and the laptop screen and Pipe B would run the DFPs. Also I don't think in this case CRT has anything to do with a CRT monitor but the actual analog port on your machine whereas LFP is the laptop screen or DFP maybe has to do with the DVI port. I would try CRT+LFP,DFP+DFP2 in both your device sections and see what happens. 

Can anybody clarify if I'm right or what exactly the MonitorLayouts are and their pipes?

Thanks,

Eric

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-06-20
> **enortham said:**
> I by no means am an expert on this so anybody feel free to ring in if I'm off base. Drenesh, I think your MonitorLayout is wrong. I think the MonitorLayout should be identical for both your device sections since it really is the same physical device; that's at least my guess. The docs at [http://www.xfree86.org/current/i810.4.html](http://www.xfree86.org/current/i810.4.html) aren't that clear for MonitorLayout but I think CRT+LFP,DFP+DFP2 means pipe A runs the CRT and the laptop screen and Pipe B would run the DFPs. Also I don't think in this case CRT has anything to do with a CRT monitor but the actual analog port on your machine whereas LFP is the laptop screen or DFP maybe has to do with the DVI port. I would try CRT+LFP,DFP+DFP2 in both your device sections and see what happens. 

Can anybody clarify if I'm right or what exactly the MonitorLayouts are and their pipes?

Thanks,

Eric

DFP = dvi ports
CRT = vga ports

also, when both vga and dvi ports are available, DVI is always the primary pipe (at least according to some of the manuals I read).

### Post by bclark on 2007-06-21
> **enortham said:**
> Thanks Veloce. I uninstalled the xorg intel drivers and reinstalled the i810 drivers and now I have dual monitors with a shared desktop. I can't believe I spent a day trying to get the other drivers to work. I don't recall seeing anyone else mention problems with them and a dual display. Oh well now the only problem is my external lcd is displaying 640x480 when it should be at 1440x900. 

I ran 
```

sudo 915resolution 50 1440 900

```

and restarted the with ctrl+alt+bkspc and the external display didn't display anything. I then restarted the computer and both displays came up in 1280x800. I ran

```

sudo 915resolution 50 1440 900

```

one more time and then restarted xserver again and now I finally have 1280x800 on one screen and 1440x900 on the other. The refresh rate doesn't look so hot but I'll check that out tomorrow. 

Let me know if you have any hints on how to set the display and card to the optimal settings.

Cheers!

Eric

anyway you could post your xorg.conf.  I would be interested in seeing any successful xinerama dual monitor layouts using i810.  i'm having a hell of a time with the onboard intel on my Dell x620

		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Multihead Layout"
	Screen 0	"Main Screen" 
	Screen 1     	"Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#Option "Xinerama" "true"
	Option "Xinerama" "On" 
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

### Post by drenesh on 2007-06-22
I got my setup working with the Intel chip and i810 Drivers. I use switchmon to switch resolutions and have 2 external monitors hooked up to my docking station. 

Just thought I'd post my xorg.conf since I'm a success story:

```

# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
        FontPath        "/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
        # path to defoma fonts
        FontPath        "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
        Load    "i2c"
        Load    "bitmap"
        Load    "ddc"
        Load    "dri"
        Load    "extmod"
        Load    "freetype"
        Load    "glx"
        Load    "int10"
        Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver          "kbd"
        Option          "CoreKeyboard"
        Option          "XkbRules"      "xorg"
        Option          "XkbModel"      "pc105"
        Option          "XkbLayout"     "us"
        Option  "XkbOptions"    "ctrl:nocaps"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Configured Mouse"
        Driver          "mouse"
        Option          "CorePointer"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/input/mice"
        Option          "Protocol"              "ImPS/2"
        Option          "ZAxisMapping"          "4 5"
        Option          "Emulate3Buttons"       "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier      "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver          "synaptics"
        Option          "SendCoreEvents"        "true"
        Option          "Device"                "/dev/psaux"
        Option          "Protocol"              "auto-dev"
        Option          "HorizScrollDelta"      "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "stylus"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "stylus"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"         # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "eraser"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "eraser"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"         # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "cursor"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"         # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
  Screen  0
  Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
EndSection

Section "Device"
  Identifier    "dev2"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
  Screen 1
  Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Generic Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
#       HorizSync       28-72
#       VertRefresh     43-60
#  DisplaySize 302 191
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
  Identifier  "mon2"
  Option    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Device          "Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Monitor         "Generic Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
  Identifier  "screen2"
  Device    "dev2"
  Monitor   "mon2"
  DefaultDepth  24
  SubSection "Display"
    Depth   24
    Modes   "1280x1024"
  EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen 0                "Default Screen"
  Screen 1    "screen2" RightOf "Default Screen"
  Option                "Xinerama" "On"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection

```


G'luck :D

	EndSubSection
EndSection

	Identifier	"Multihead Layout"
	Screen 0	"Main Screen"
	Screen 1	"Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	#Screen 1 	"Second Screen" LeftOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"	
	Option "Xinerama" "On" 
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

### Post by Ziox on 2007-06-23
> **bclark said:**
> Sorry to be owning this thread, but i hope my success may help someone and i have one final question. 

I finally got dual monitor support using xinerama with my oboard intel for my Dell GX620.  I had to completely remove beryl and compiz.  Do I have to do that, I miss the desktop effects?

[/code]

I believe that in order for Desktop Effects to function, your computer must have direct rendering enabled.  However, the current Xinerama extension disables direct rendering.  So, unfortunately, you cannot have Xinerama and XGL/AIGLX enabled at the same time. (though you can always try).  The Xinerama extension could've been updated since I wrote this guide.

---

        Modes      "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection

        Depth       15

    EndSubSection

        Modes      "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Second Screen"
    Device         "nVidia Corporation C51 [GeForce 6150 LE]"
    Monitor        "1 hp f1503"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBVisuals" "True"
    Option         "NoLogo" "True"
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1280x1024" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection

   EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1280x1024" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection
```

Thanks in advance :)

---

### Post by Ziox on 2007-06-24
are you attempting to use both cards for dual monitor?

if so, then your xorg.conf file isn't configured correctly.  Currently the card is only using the Nvidia card.

If you want to use the ATI card as well, change one of the Device section to ATI and the driver to either ati or fglrx.

Also, get dual monitor setup first before enabling Beryl.

---

### Post by bclark on 2007-06-24
could you post some of the error lines from your Xorg.0.log file.  Just run 'grep -n EE Xorg.0.log' and post those lines.  Also have you tried to removing beryl?

---

### Post by enortham on 2007-06-24
> **bclark said:**
> anyway you could post your xorg.conf.  I would be interested in seeing any successful xinerama dual monitor layouts using i810.  i'm having a hell of a time with the onboard intel on my Dell x620

I don't know if it will help but here is an xorg.conf file that works with the intel drivers and dual monitors; one on my laptop and the other an external lcd.

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen		0
	#Option 		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option          "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
        Option          "DRI" "false"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Driver          "i810"
        BusID           "PCI:0:2:0"
        Screen          1
	#Option          "DDCMode" "True"
        Option          "MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
        Option          "DRI" "true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"0 Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	#HorizSync	28-64
	#VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "1 Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        #HorizSync      28-64
        #VertRefresh    43-60
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"0 Screen"
	Device		"0 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Monitor		"0 Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "1 Screen"
        Device          "1 Intel Corporation Mobile 945GM/GMS/940GML Express Integrated Graphics Controller"
        Monitor         "1 Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24

        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           32
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection

	SubSection "Display"
                Depth           4
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection

        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           8
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection

	SubSection "Display"
                Depth           15
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection

        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           24
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection

        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           32
                Modes           "1440x900"
        EndSubSection

EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "0 Screen"
        Screen          1 "1 Screen" RightOf "0 Screen"
	Option   	"Xinerama" "on"
	Option		"Clone" "off"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

Option "SWCursor" "true"

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"

EndSection

	Identifier	"stylus"

EndSection

	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by RyCS42 on 2007-07-06
Ok, a small update. I fixed the cursor issue by switching the Device section that the "SWCursor" option was under to the first one instead of the one used by the second monitor. I've now actually got a cursor on my second monitor.

However, now my mouse tends to leave a trail wherever it goes, and it isnt removed until something updates the screen at the particular location that the mouse trail is. 

This is very strange, because on my last installation of Feisty (on this very same laptop), it all worked fine, Xinerama and everything. Ever since I've reinstalled, though, it's been fighting me for every little thing.

---

### Post by mregecko on 2007-07-10
Thanks for posting this How-To! I'm working on getting this working on an Acer 1640 laptop... it has an Intel On-Board video controller.

I followed the How-To (I think) pretty well... I've done this on other computers, but can't seem to get it with this laptop!

Primary should be the internal flat panel, secondary should be the external (VGA adapter) flat panel.

Here's the xorg.conf file I've got... After I restart X, the screen just goes black. I have to ctrl+alt+del to get a reboot. Nothing works (no ctrl+alt+f1, etc... nothing). Only ctrl+alt+del

```
# Xorg configuration created by system-config-display

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "single head configuration"
	Screen      0  "Main Screen"
	Screen	    1  "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	InputDevice    "Synaptics" "CorePointer"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

	Identifier  "Keyboard0"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "yes"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Main Monitor"
	ModelName    "LCD Panel 1280x800"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 90.0
	VertRefresh  59.9 - 60.1
	Option	    "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "Second Monitor"
        ModelName    "LCD Panel 1280x1024"
        HorizSync    31.5 - 90.0
        VertRefresh  59.9 - 60.1
        Option      "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "0 Videocard0"
	Driver      "intel"
	Screen	0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,CRT"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "1 Videocard0"
        Driver      "intel"
	Screen	1
        Option "DDCMode" "True"
        Option "MonitorLayout" "LFP,CRT"
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Main Screen"
	Device     "0 Videocard0"
	Monitor    "Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Second Screen"
        Device     "1 Videocard0"
        Monitor    "Second Monitor"
        DefaultDepth     24
        SubSection "Display"
                Viewport   0 0
                Depth     24
        EndSubSection
EndSection

```

Thanks in advance for your help...

-- Will

### Post by olafbooij on 2007-07-10
Nice HOWTO!

However, I should warn that using the tv-out of an NVIDIA-card does not work with the open source nv-drivers (correct me if I'm wrong!!). This will result in an error : 
"Fatal server error: Requested Entity already in use!".

To use two monitors (or monitor and tv) with one NVIDIA-card you need to install the proprietary (binary) nvidia drivers.

Greets,
Olaf

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	1
	Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	4
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	8
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	15
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	16
			Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"1 Second Screen"
	Device		"1 Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"1 Generic Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	1
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	4
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	8
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	15
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	16
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1152x864"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  	screen 0 "0 Main Screen"
	screen 1 "1 Second Screen" RightOf "0 Main Screen"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Inputdevice	"stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection
```

### Post by Ziox on 2007-07-21
Xinerama disables Direct Rendering.  In the current Xorg version, it is IMPOSSIBLE to enable DR with Xinerama.

---

### Post by Darmak on 2007-07-22

> Xinerama disables Direct Rendering.  In the current Xorg version, it is IMPOSSIBLE to enable DR with Xinerama.

Ahh nuts.  Well, I guess I'll go back to how I had my dual monitors set up, thanks anyways.  This is a really easy to use How-To and I actually enjoyed the process.  

Thanks once again.

### Post by james.wilde on 2007-08-01
Thanks, Ziox, for all your work on this.

HP desktop, G450, twin Dell E172FPt monitors is my setup.  Got it to work with the following exception:  in the ServerLayout section I have left out the two lines 

        Screen      0  "Main Screen"
	Screen	    1  "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"

If I include these, I get two screens showing the Ubuntu background (sandy coloured) but no login window.

Additionally the TMDS port appears to be the primary port, and the VGA port the secondary.  At least, my TMDS port is the left screen with the following xorg.conf file (relevant sections only):

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "0 Matrox Graphics, Inc. MGA G400/G450"
        Driver          "mga"
        BusID           "PCI:6:0:0"
        Option          "OldDmaInit"            "True"
        Screen          0
Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "1 Matrox Graphics, Inc. MGA G400/G450"
        Driver          "mga"
        BusID           "PCI:6:0:0"
        Option          "OldDmaInit"            "True"
        Screen          1
Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "0 Generic Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       31-80
        VertRefresh     56-76
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "1 Generic Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       31-80
        VertRefresh     56-76
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "0 Default Screen"
        Device          "0 Matrox Graphics, Inc. MGA G400/G450"
        Monitor         "0 Generic Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
 ...

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "1 Default Screen"
        Device          "1 Matrox Graphics, Inc. MGA G400/G450"
        Monitor         "1 Generic Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Depth           1
                Modes           "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
        EndSubSection
 ...

Section "ServerLayout"
        Identifier      "Default Layout"
        Screen          "0 Default Screen"
        Screen          "1 Default Screen" RightOf "0 Default Screen"
        Option          "Xinerama"      "True"
        InputDevice     "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice     "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
        Mode    0666
EndSection

Anyway, thanks a bunch!

//James

```

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"

	Option		"CorePointer"

	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"

Section "Device"

	BusID		"PCI:1:4:0"

	Identifier	"COMPAQ 7550"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-75
	VertRefresh	50-85
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"HP Pavalion"

	HorizSync	30-75
	VertRefresh	50-85
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"

	Monitor		"COMPAQ 7550"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"

EndSection

	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Option		"Xinerama" "True"

```

> # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  	Screen 	      0 "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	Screen 	      1 "aticonfig-Screen[1]" RightOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Inputdevice	"stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

	# path to defoma fonts

### Post by daicoden on 2007-09-02
:confused:

I have been reading different posts and after all that I finally think I have something that is so close!

Here is my config file

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc101"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"device[0]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
	Screen 		0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"device[1]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:1"
	Screen		1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"monitor[0]"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-70
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"monitor[1]"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-70
	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"screen[0]"
	Device		"device[0]"
	Monitor		"monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:2:0:0"
	Screen      0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
	Driver		"nv"
	BusID		"PCI:5:0:0"
	Screen      1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT, CRT"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"0 Default Screen"
	Device		"0 NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"1 Default Screen"
	Device		"1 NVIDIA Corporation NV43 [GeForce 6600 GT]"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen	      1 "Main Monitor"
	Screen        0 "Second Monitor" RightOf "Main Monitor"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

I think your error is in the last section.  In the two "Screen" sections, you have the identifiers of your two screens as "0 Default Screen" and "1 Default Screen".  These are what you should put in the two "Screen" lines in the "ServerLayout" section.  The names you put are the identifiers for the "Monitors" rather than the "Screens".  It's very confusing, I know.  I'm not sure the difference between a monitor and a screen, but the name of the screen is what is needed here. :)

Edit: I just realized how long this thread is and everything, so perhaps this has been answered already, haha. :rolleyes:

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-04
Try adjusting the refresh rates on the monitors to match what the manufacture recomends. Off the top of my head I would guess the samsung to be in the 30-80 hsync range and 55-75 in the vsync range. The nec should prolly be the same try googleing them and see what you come up with.

EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by chessercizes on 2007-09-08
Hey all.
Firstly, thank you very much for this guide (and your others, the bigdesktop one works wonders for me), and thank you soo much to all of you guys helping everyone out (and withstanding our bombardment of xorg.conf files).That's why I love these forums so much =)

(BTW, I have an ATI Radeon 9600 with the fglrx driver)


Originally, I used ATI Big-Desktop. This worked fine, but the problem was that fullscreening anything filled up both screens. I attempted to find a solution to this problem, but was unable to do so. 
Then, I followed this guide to get xinerama working, but it didn't work at all with XGL / Compiz Fusion. 
I googled it and found this:
[http://forum.compiz-fusion.org/showthread.php?p=26481]("http://forum.compiz-fusion.org/showthread.php?p=26481")
which said to change the startxgl.sh file, but that yielded no results either.

After these failed attempts, i just reverted back it ATI Big-Desktop.

Now, somehow, fullscreening only fills up one screen, and it works very very well in XGL!! 
Im guessing that the change in the startxgl.sh file is the reason for this working now (because i just reverted to an old xorg.conf file, and didn't change the startxgl.sh file back to how it was).

So for anyone else who wants to try this out heres how:

*NOTE* THIS IS ONLY FOR ATI CARDS + FGLRX 
ALSO, I CAN PROVIDE NO GUARANTEE THAT THIS WILL WORK
[SIZE="3"]1) ATI Big-Desktop[/SIZE]
Follow these instructions to get ATI Big Desktop:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=301941&]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=301941&")

It will probably be easier if you edit a xorg.conf file that you haven't tried to get xinerama working, purely because you make different changes, and so instead of attempting to undo everything you did, if you just make a copy of an old xorg.conf that hasn't been messed with, then it will make your life easier =)

*NOTE* BE VERY VERY SURE THAT YOU BACK UP YOUR xorg.conf FILE (its the first step in that guide)

[SIZE="3"] 2) Change Your Startxgl.sh[/SIZE]

After successfully enabling ATI Big-Desktop, you'll need to change your startxgl.sh

[SIZE="2"]a) First and FOREMOST: Back up your startxgl.sh file [/SIZE]

CD (change directory) into the directory where your startxgl.sh file is (for me it is /usr/local/bin)
```
cd <directory with startxgl>
``` (replacing "<directory with startxgl>" with the path to the folder)
then back it up:
```
sudo cp startxgl.sh startxgl.sh.backup
```

[SIZE="2"]b) Second: Change your startxgl.sh file[/SIZE]

Ok, so now do:
```
sudo gedit startxgl.sh
```

and when that opens, replace yours with the following:

```

#!/bin/sh
Xgl :1 -fullscreen -ac -br -accel xv:fbo -accel glx:pbuffer **+xinerama & sleep 2 && DISPLAY=:1** &
DISPLAY=:1
cookie="$(xauth -i nextract - :0 | cut -d ' ' -f 9)"
xauth -i add :1 . "$cookie"
exec dbus-launch --exit-with-session gnome-session

```

The part in bold represents what i had to change, and most likely what you will have to add.

*note* I didn't come up with this change, it is from this forum:
[http://forum.compiz-fusion.org/showthread.php?t=3826&page=2]("http://forum.compiz-fusion.org/showthread.php?t=3826&page=2")


Then, reboot and see how it works.

I really really hope this works for anyone who has been having problems. I'm not even sure what this does, just know it woked for me =D. I'm not very knowledgeable at all about linux, and stumbled upon this completely by chance. I just wanted to share what worked for me, in a hope that maybe someone with a similar problem could benefit =).

Please tell me if i forgot something, something is wrong, or even if this is completely useless. Oh, and please do tell how it works for you!!

=)

btw i attached my xorg.conf and startxgl.sh if you need to see them

---

### Post by graben3 on 2007-09-12
I searched around the web on forums how to get my 2 video cards (both connected to one screen) working like in windows XP. I finally found something all of you can be happy to find : 
displayconfig-gtk

This is a small utility for ubutu that is working on feisty fawn. You can download the binary packages from :
[http://glatzor.de/filesink/displayconfig/feisty](http://glatzor.de/filesink/displayconfig/feisty)

Install the guidance backends package first then the other one.

then, run 
sudo displayconfig-gtk
in a ternimal window.

The utility pop up and you can test the configurations you want. I got one configuration working well with this so I saved the location.

Now the program just creates a xorg.conf file but does not write it to the /etc/X11/xorg.conf file.

Just get the configuration file generated at :
/var/lib/displayconfig-gtk/locations

then copy it over your existing /etc/X11/xorg.conf file and hit ctrl+alt+backspace to restart X server.

Everything should now work as the testmode in the utility. IE. if your 2 screens were working in test mode it should now too out of test mode.



Here is the output generated by this program for my xorg.conf file :
I Have a Nvidia FX 5200 and a Nvidia Riva TNT2 64 card. Both are on a LCD display which handles a resolution of 1280x1024

I removed from the output file the monitor resolutions I didn't want. The file was a bit longer unmodified but It still work with my 2 screens





# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Nov  9 17:55:20 PST 2006

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg


Section "Files"
	
	# path to defoma fonts
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	Fontpath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load		"bitmap"
	Load		"ddc"
	Load		"extmod"
	Load		"freetype"
	Load		"int10"
	Load		"vbe"

	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"VS"
	Vendorname	"Generic CRT Display"
	Modelname	"Monitor 1280x1024"
	Horizsync	31.5-79.0
	Vertrefresh	50-90
  modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"LG"
	Vendorname	"Generic CRT Display"
	Modelname	"Monitor 1280x1024"
	Horizsync	31.5-79.0
	Vertrefresh	50-90
  modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier	"TNT2"
	Boardname	"RIVA TNT2"
	Busid		"PCI:2:11:0"

	Screen	0
	Vendorname	"RIVA"

##These affects the server loading or behavior. Probably doest the same as the startx command line options

	# Starts Xinerama automatically at XServer startup  
	Option		"Xinerama"	"true"
EndSection

Section "device" # 
	Identifier	"device1"
	Boardname	"NVIDIA GeForce FX (generic)"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Screen	1
	Vendorname	"NVIDIA"
EndSection

	Defaultdepth	24

	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1280x1024@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "monitor" # 
	Identifier	"monitor1"
	Vendorname	"Generic CRT Display"
	Modelname	"Monitor 1280x1024"
	Horizsync	31.5-79.0
	Vertrefresh	50-90
modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection


##end of file


Here is the output from lspci : 
lspci |grep VGA

Output :
01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: nVidia Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX
5200] (rev a1)
02:0b.0 VGA compatible controller: nVidia Corporation NV5M64 [RIVA TNT2
Model 64/Model 64 Pro] (rev 15)

Notice how the 02:0b.0 become 2:11:0


I hope it can help some others like me who found that getting 2 monitors to work on 2 screens with 2 graphic cards is a pain the *** on linux. It finally took me about 3 years installing and reinstalling linux from time to time to get this working trying each time i installed linux  !

Now it is ok.... ouf ! nice !

    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Second Screen"
    Device         "1 nVidia Corporation G70 [GeForce 7600 GS]"
    Monitor        "Second Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "NoLogo" "True"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       1
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       4

    EndSubSection
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1024x768" "800x600"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Disable"
EndSection

### Post by hanzj on 2007-09-22
Ok. I've got dual monitor setup working with Xinerema. Thanks. 


But I've got a question: 

My two monitors are of diferent models, sizes, and optimal resolutions and frequencies. Should not the xorg.conf file reflect this? My first monitor is NEC MultiSync FP2141SB. The manual says that it has a diagonal of 22 inch / 55 cm, and a viewabel image size of 20 inch / 508 mm. The manual recommends 1600 x 1200 @ 85 Hz, but it can go as high as 2048 x1536 @ 50 to 86 Hz, according to the manual. My second monitor is an AOC (Model No: 9Klr). I don't have the manual for this second monitor, but according to some stuff I've found on the internet, it's a 19 inch flat CRT monitor with a m]aximum resolution of 1,600 x 1,200 ([http://www.aocdisplay.com/epius/021202b.html](http://www.aocdisplay.com/epius/021202b.html)).

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-22
If you are using Xinerama, it requires both of your screens to have the same bit depth. This could be causing your second screen to black out. Have you looked into trying MergedFB options?

---

### Post by hanzj on 2007-09-22
Scorziello,
My screens don't black out (anymore).
What do you mean by "same bit-depth".

---

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-22
Bit depth has to do with how many colors your monitor can handle. In your xorg.conf file they are labled 1,4,8,15,16,24. If you are seeing things correctly on your monitor's, such as they are both in the correct resolution, and the colors look the way they should I would not worry about how it is layed out in your xorg.conf file.

What I have found with Xinerama is that if you are using two different monitors with different depths and resolutions it will usually pick the lower of the two resolutions and apply it to both monitors.

---

### Post by dawsdesign on 2007-09-24
Cancel that, I got it working with the following configuration:
```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Video 1"
	Screen		0
	Option 		"DDCMode" 	"True"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "DFP,CRT"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Video 2"
	Option 		"DevicePresence" "on"
	Screen 		1
	Option 		"DDCMode" 	"True"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "DFP,CRT"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor 1"
	Option 		"DevicePresence" "on"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor 2"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen 1"
	Device		"Intel Video 1"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor 1"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen 2"
	Device		"Intel Video 2"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor 2"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0	"Default Screen 1"
	Screen       	1   	"Default Screen 2" RightOf "Default Screen 1"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Option 		"Xinerama" 	"true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

Anyone know what might be the cause of this?

If I disable one of my screens, <CTRL>+<ALT>+F1 works fine. But that's quite an elaborate process to go through every time I want text-mode. Also, the blank screen is "functional" in that I can log into the system in text mode... only I can't see a thing.

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-24
MIne does the same thing. I have a feeling its because dual screens is part of your xserver and when you are in "text mode" you don't use the xserver. So you have to physically tell the system what you want it to do.

	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

#Device 1
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies Inc Radeon RV100 QY [Radeon 7000/VE]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option 		"DDCMode" 	"True"
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
EndSection

#Device2
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies Inc Radeon RV100 QY [Radeon 7000/VE]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		1
	Option 		"DDCMode" 	"True"
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
EndSection

#Monitor1
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

#Monitor2
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

#Screen1
Section "Screen"
	Identifier   	"Main Screen"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies Inc Radeon RV100 QY [Radeon 7000/VE]
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

#Screen2
Section "Screen"
	Identifier   	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies Inc Radeon RV100 QY [Radeon 7000/VE]
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0	"Main Screen"
	Screen		1	"Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Option		"Xinerama"	"true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

### Post by veloce on 2007-09-26
> **HuskyDev said:**
> 	
	Option 		"MonitorLayout"	"LFP,LFP2"


Change LFP2 to CRT.  (unless your laptop has two internal monitors)

(or to DFP if it is connected by DVI)

Cheers
Veloce

---

### Post by HuskyDev on 2007-09-26
> **veloce said:**
> Change LFP2 to CRT.  (unless your laptop has two internal monitors)

(or to DFP if it is connected by DVI)

Cheers
Veloce

Is that also true for the case where my external monitor is also LCD?

Thanks for the reply!

---

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-26
EIEdwards....There should not be any reference to screens in your device section. You have screen 0 and 1 in both of your device sections. These sections are for telling your xserver what driver to use to run your hardware. Other than that it looks ok.

---

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-26
HuskyDev that is true even if you have a LCD. It is just telling the driver where the signal needs to go. LFP stands for local flat panel, which is your built in screen. CRT is for anything attached to the VGA output of your video card.

---

### Post by ElEdwards on 2007-09-26
> There should not be any reference to screens in your device section. You have screen 0 and 1 in both of your device sections. These sections are for telling your xserver what driver to use to run your hardware. Other than that it looks ok.

Scorziello,
I did that based on the original posting on this thread.  That does indeed appear to cause problems.  I removed those "screen" references in the "device" sections....but now I'm back to two duplicate displays. :(

Any further ideas?
Thanks :)

---

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-26
#Device 1
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies Inc Radeon RV100 QY [Radeon 7000/VE]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option 		"DDCMode" 	"True"
	Option 		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"
EndSection

You can try and label your monitor layout option "TDMS,TDMS2". Other than that I don't know what else to try.
