### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-26
I'm sorry, I just copied from your xorg file that you had posted earlier. Wasn't trying to confuse you.

I was just looking over your xorg again and I noticed that you have labeled your graphics card with two different names. Is this because you physically have two cards? Because if not that might also have something to do with it. The Xserver might be looking for two separate cards. You might want to try labeling them the same. I wish I could be of more help but I am still some what of a noob. Let me know how it goes.

### Post by scorziello on 2007-09-27
What I had to do with my intel driver was to tell xorg a virtual size to run. 
```
SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Virtual  2560   1024
	EndSubSection
```
Basically you need to add up the two resolutions that you have. In my case I have two monitors, the laptop monitor is running at 1280x800 and the external that I have runs at 1280x1024. I didn't edit on all of the depth lines because I only use 24,

> 
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	Fontpath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts

EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load		"i2c"
	Load		"bitmap"
	Load		"ddc"
	Load		"dri"
	Load		"extmod"
	Load		"freetype"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"int10"
	Load		"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"tr"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	Horizsync	28-64
	Vertrefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	1
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	4
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	8
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	15
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	16
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Inputdevice	"stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection

### Post by gerdt on 2007-09-28
Hi, I have the exact same problem as [*_**TheCat (reply #11)**_*]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1815939&postcount=11") and [*_**bdean42 (reply #26)**_*]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1886193&postcount=26"), but there is no answer to their (and my) problem to be found in this entire thread. I was wondering if someone knows the answer to this problem.

To summarize my problem: I have succesfully (thanks to this great HOWTO) managed to configure Xinerama on my Radeon 9600 XT with two TFT monitors (Samsung SyncMaster 920N-19"- & 720N-17"-). But when I move my mouse cursor into the second monitor the cursor won't change. The cursor stays exactly like when it left the first monitor. 

> **TheCat said:**
> I've got an ATI Radeon 9000 Pro (VGA and DVI outputs) and two Samsung SyncMaster 710N 17" LCD panels.  I'm using the fglrx driver.  Xinerama seems to be working fine, but on the secondary monitor, the mouse cursor won't change -- it's totally static as whatever it was displaying when I moved it from the primary screen to the secondary screen.  For example, most of the time, it's a regular arrow, and in Firefox, it won't change to a hand.  When resizing a window, it won't change to a targeting cursor.  It stays an arrow.  If it happened to be displayed as a hand when I moved it to the secondary screen, it continues to display as a hand.  The cursor also seems to 'jump' a bit when it hits transition points, like the edge of a window.  This is more of an annoyance than a serious problem (it's a bit disconcerting), but I wondered if anyone had encountered it or could suggest a solution.


> **bdean42 said:**
> I'm having this same problem and cannot seem to find a solution. my configuration is a laptop and a CRT for the monitors. xinerama works fine but the mouse cursors don't work on the second display. also, i don't know if this makes any difference (i doubt it) but i can't get DRI to work with the fglrx drivers (composite is turned off). i should disable xinerama and see if it is affecting this DRI problem too.

but to me, the DRI issue is more an annoyance than anything.  the main problem is getting these cursors to work correctly.  constantly having the wrong mouse cursor in firefox or any app really makes it very hard to click on things. especially if you get stuck with the text selection cursor :-?

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Main Screen"
	Screen      1  "Secondary Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"
	Load  "ddc"
	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"
	Load  "int10"
	Load  "type1"
	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option "AIGLX" "off"
	Option "blank time" "0"
	Option "standby time" "0"
	Option "suspend time" "0"
	Option "off time" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc104"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"

EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # /dev/input/event

	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "cursor"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "SyncMaster 920N"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "SyncMaster 720N"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AR [Radeon 9600 XT]"
#	Driver		"ati"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		0
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AR [Radeon 9600 XT]"
#	Driver		"ati"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen		1
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "TMDS, TMDS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Main Screen"
	Device     "0 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AR [Radeon 9600 XT]"
	Monitor    "SyncMaster 920N"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Secondary Screen"
	Device     "1 ATI Technologies, Inc. RV350 AR [Radeon 9600 XT]"
	Monitor    "SyncMaster 720N"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
  Option "Composite" "0" 
EndSection
```

PS: I had to change the Device-driver of both Devices into fglrx in order to get my xinerama configuration working (see my xorg.conf).

I believe that Xinerama does not support more than 2 screens (i.e. Screen 0 and Screen 1 only).  Therefore, you need to combine Xinerama and Twinview together to link all three together.

Basic setup as follows: link two monitors under one screen section using TwinView, then link the said section with the last monitor.

Note: I've never done this before, so I cannot guarantee that it'll succeed.

Good Luck!

### Post by gerdt on 2007-10-20
As for my earlier question/problem, I finally solved it.
I was waiting for the official release of gutsy to do a fresh install of my computer. I was hoping that the graphical interface of "Screens and Graphics" of Gutsy would solve my problem. Unfortunately this was not the case, so I followed this manual again.

So I did two things: I first installed the ati drivers which I downloaded from ati.amd.com, and I configured Xinerama by editing xorg.conf. And now, under Gutsy instead of Feisty, my mouse works just fine using Xinerama! In short, my solution was upgrading to Gutsy!

> **gerdt said:**
> Hi, I have the exact same problem as [*_**TheCat (reply #11)**_*]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1815939&postcount=11") and [*_**bdean42 (reply #26)**_*]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1886193&postcount=26"), but there is no answer to their (and my) problem to be found in this entire thread. I was wondering if someone knows the answer to this problem.

To summarize my problem: I have succesfully (thanks to this great HOWTO) managed to configure Xinerama on my Radeon 9600 XT with two TFT monitors (Samsung SyncMaster 920N-19"- & 720N-17"-). But when I move my mouse cursor into the second monitor the cursor won't change. The cursor stays exactly like when it left the first monitor. 

```

...
see [*_**original post (# 328)**_*]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=1815939&postcount=11")
...

```

PS: I had to change the Device-driver of both Devices into fglrx in order to get my xinerama configuration working (see my xorg.conf).

My xorg.conf:

```

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "stylus"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "stylus"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "eraser"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "eraser"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "cursor"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Screen		0
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS,LVDS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Screen		1
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS,LVDS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"SyncMaster 920N"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"SyncMaster 720N"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-81
	VertRefresh	56-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 0"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor		"SyncMaster 920N"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 1"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor		"SyncMaster 720N"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Screen 0"
	Screen		1 "Screen 1" RightOf "Screen 0"
	Option		"Xinerama" "true"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

```

EDIT: I said the solution was upgrading to Gutsy, but I am starting to believe that the real solution to the corrupt mouse pointer was installing the ati driver from [http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html]("http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html")

---

### Post by Stop-state-terrorism on 2007-10-21
**Well i got it to work !**

i just went to xserver ... did seperate screen and checked the box Xinerama ! and it works !
2 screen, maximizing works great , and all is good !

thanks !

my code (which makes sense now that i read it ;) )

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Sep 12 14:30:30 PDT 2007


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 1680 0
    Screen      1  "Screen1" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"

# Removed Option "Xinerama" "true"
    Option         "Xinerama" "1"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Samsung SyncMaster"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 81.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor1"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "LG L226W"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 83.0
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7900 GT/GTO"
    Option         "DDCMode" "True"
    Option         "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard1"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7900 GT/GTO"
    Option         "DDCMode" "True"
    Option         "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor"
    BusID          "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen          1
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "Twinview" "1"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "DFP: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard1"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "Twinview" "1"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "CRT: nvidia-auto-select +1680+0"
    Identifier     "Screen1"
    Device         "Videocard0"

    Option         "TwinView" "0"

EndSection

### Post by SeeFurstAskl8r on 2007-10-22
Hey there... I just installed the ATI drivers manually for my FireGL 5100 and I tried to use this configuration for Xinerama is it's a no go, I am using Gutsy and I'm wondering if more devleopment needs be done for this version. My displays are exactly the same.. two Acer AL1916 monitors. At this point they just display the same thing.

there are a few things that are weird about my set up which I think is why this is not working. 

first of all for each section Device, Monitor and Screen, there are multiple definitions. One seems to be the default, then there seems to be the definitions written by aticonfig whose Identifiers are  "aticonfig-Device[0]", "aticonfig-Monitor[0]" and "aticonfig-Screen[0]" respectively. The ServerLayout section lists aticonfig-Screen[0] as the identifier for the screen it is to load but the line is interesting.. It has two extra zeros after it.. like this:

```
Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
```

so I changed it to this:

```
Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
Screen	    1  "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]"

```

Here is my xorg.conf file:

```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Option 	       "Xinerama" "true"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	Screen	    1  "aticonfig-Screen[1]" LeftOf "aticonfig-Screen[0]"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

EndSection

	Load  "v4l"

	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"

	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"

	HorizSync    30.0 - 82.0
	VertRefresh  56.0 - 76.0

	ModeLine     "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
	ModeLine     "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync

EndSection

 #

	Gamma        1

	Option "MonitorLayout" "TMDS,TMDS"

	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth     24
		Modes    "800x600@60" "832x624@75" "800x600@75" "1024x768@75" "800x600@72" "1024x768@70" "800x600@56" "1024x768@60" "640x480@75" "1152x864@75" "640x480@72" "1280x1024@75" "640x480@60" "1280x960@60" "1280x1024@60" "1280x960@75" "1400x1050@60" "1400x1050@75" "1600x1200@65" "1600x1200@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"

 #

	Monitor    "monitor1"
	DefaultDepth     24
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]"

	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection

	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

```

So I'm wondering if I am copying the right entries and if the ati stuff is just 3d accleration devices/drivers etc? Should I be copying the other entries?

Another interesting thing is that when I check to see which VGA devices I have:  ```
 lspci -x | grep VGA
01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc R423 [FireGL V5100 (PCIE)] (rev 80)

```

BUT When I grep ATI I get:
```

lspci -x | grep ATI
01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: ATI Technologies Inc R423 [FireGL V5100 (PCIE)] (rev 80)
01:00.1 Display controller: ATI Technologies Inc R423GL-SE [FireGL V5100 (PCIE)] (Secondary) (rev 80)

```

Should I indicate the PCI bus Id of the section entry somewhere? All device entries are using something inherited from somewhere I reckon. I think it's inheriting from Failsafe Device or something? is there an inheritance scheme?

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

```

### Post by gerdt on 2007-10-28
@airtonix

Perhaps I can help you. I also have a Radeon 9600.

What I did was:

1) follow instructions from [[COLOR="Blue"]_here_[/COLOR]]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=221174")
(sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg)

2) download and install the driver from ([[COLOR="Blue"]_http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html_[/COLOR]]("http://ati.amd.com/support/drivers/linux/linux-radeon.html")) 

3)follow the instructions from this [[COLOR="Blue"]_Xinerama how-to_[/COLOR]]("http://www.ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=1773624").

Without this driver I also managed a Xinerama dual screen, but with a corrupt mouse pointer in the second screen. This driver solved this for me.

This is my final xorg.conf, I hope it helps you:

```


Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "stylus"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "stylus"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "eraser"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "eraser"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Driver          "wacom"
        Identifier      "cursor"
        Option          "Device"        "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option          "Type"          "cursor"
        Option          "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Screen		0
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS,LVDS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
	Screen		1
	Option		"DDCMode" "True"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS,LVDS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"SyncMaster 920N"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"SyncMaster 720N"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-81
	VertRefresh	56-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 0"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor		"SyncMaster 920N"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 1"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
	Monitor		"SyncMaster 720N"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Screen 0"
	Screen		1 "Screen 1" RightOf "Screen 0"
	Option		"Xinerama" "true"

	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

```

### Post by gerdt on 2007-10-28
@airtonix, BTW:

I saw in your xorg.conf that you commented a line which I believe should be uncommented. I believe that's the reason why you are cloning your screen instead of expanding it:

> **airtonix said:**
> ...

```
...

Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "Default Layout"
Screen 0 "Screen 1"
**# Screen 1 "Screen 2" LeftOf "Screen 1"**
Option "Xinerama" "true"
InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
InputDevice "Configured Mouse"

...
```

...

### Post by Ziox on 2007-10-28
First of all, the GUI in Gutsy is rudimentary at best.  It'll get better eventually.  Follow this guide.

Next, after applying Xinerama, you CANNOT use Compiz, because of the lack of 3D rendering.

Use the MergedFB instead if you have the open source driver installed and working

### Post by gerdt on 2007-10-28
@krisfrajer
I checked you xorg.conf and I noticed two things:
I am no expert, but I saw you include the module glx. I don't know exactly what it means, but I too have a Radeon 9600 (with Xinerama working correctly) and I don't have it in my xorg.conf.

The second thing I saw is this:

you defined your second monitor:
```
Section "Monitor"
	**Identifier	"Nec"**
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	33-60
	VertRefresh	57-75
EndSection

```

and your second screen:
```
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies Inc RV350 AR [Radeon 9600]"
**	Monitor		"NEC"**
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024"	"1024x768"	"832x624"	"800x600"	"720x400"	"640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
```

As I said, I'm no expert, so I don't know if this is the cause to your problem, but:
the identifier of your monitor is **Nec**, but later you refer to this monitor by **NEC**.

I recommend you don't write Nec in capitals...

PS: As I have the same video card, you might want to check my xorg.conf to compare. I posted it a few posts earlier.

---

Thxs for pointing that out. I have review all those lines in the last 8 hours and I overlooked the uppercase in the identifier section. Anyways I corrected this and I also unable that module I was loading and it did not changed my situation :(

One thing I forgot to mention in my post is that I have commented out the line 
# screen 1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"

because every time I activate this line and I restart my display, my dispaly always gets rebooted in "low resolution mode".
That only happens when I activate that line. I have tested each monitor by itself and they work in high resolution mode but when I put them togetherit always get into the "low resolution mode" and the second monitor blanks out before outputing the "No signal" screen.

### Post by gerdt on 2007-10-29
my xorg.conf file is one generated automatically and later modified by me on the instructions I received on this forum. I didn't include anything myself except for those things presented in this topic. So the modules with which you are not familiar with were not put in there by me...

I'm no expert, so the only thing I can do is give hunches...
Why don't you remove all the modes in you screen except for the ones you want your monitor to use? Perhaps you should begin by putting both monitors on 800x600 to check if you at least get Xinerama up and running at low resolution.

Second, I recommend that you uncomment 
# screen 1 "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
and give it a shot now that you replaced NEC by Nec.
If this line is uncommented, it is logical your second screen is blank as you are telling the video card/server layout that he should only use screen 0.
My interpretation of the xorg.conf file (someone correct me if I'm wrong) is that you can define as many screen, monitors and devices as you want, but only that which is put into the section "ServerLayout" is being used by xorg.

I now realise that I put
Option		"MonitorLayout" "LVDS,LVDS"
in my xorg.conf. I don't know why I did this, because I use desktop monitors, not laptop monitors. But it works for me ... I don't know if this could be the problem? (It's just a hunch)

By the way, I installed the drivers from ati.amd.com (as you can read in my earlier post). Perhaps this helps you?

So my advice is to experiment. Make step by step, small modifications of your xorg.conf, and use the Ctrl. Alt. Backspace to check whether your new configuration is working or not.

---

### Post by gerdt on 2007-10-29
PD: are you sure the HorizSync & VertRefresh of your monitors are correctly defined. Perhaps thats the reason your monitor won't work correctly at higher resolutions. You can normally find de correct details on the manufactureres website.

---

	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"dri"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSectio

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"intel"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen      	0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" #replace primary monitor with the following:
	Ati Driver:
	NONE   -- Not connected
	CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
	TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
	LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

	i810 Driver:
	CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
	LFP -- Local Flat Panel
	DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
	TV
	CRT2
	LFP2
	DFP2
	TV2
	NONE -- Not connected
EndSection

Section "Device"    
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc Rage 128 PD/PRO TMDS"
	Driver		"ati"
	Busid		"PCI:1:1:0"
	Screen      	1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" #replace primary monitor with the following:
	Ati Driver:
	NONE   -- Not connected
	CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
	TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
	LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

	i810 Driver:
	CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
	LFP -- Local Flat Panel
	DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
	TV
	CRT2
	LFP2
	DFP2
	TV2
	NONE -- Not connected
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Main Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Monitor		"Main Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
	Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc Rage 128 PD/PRO TMDS"
	Monitor		"Second Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
	Modes		"1280x1024"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "Main Screen"
	Screen       1   "Second Screen" RightOf "Main Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection
```

---

### Post by gerdt on 2007-11-06
> ```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"intel"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen      	0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" #replace primary monitor with the following:
	Ati Driver:
	NONE   -- Not connected
	CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
	TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
	LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

	i810 Driver:
	CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
	LFP -- Local Flat Panel
	DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
	TV
	CRT2
	LFP2
	DFP2
	TV2
	NONE -- Not connected
EndSection
```

this is one error I found in your xorg.conf. You have to choose. So in the case that you have two Desktop flat panel monitors, the outcome should be something like this:


```
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"intel"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen      	0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" **"TMDS,TMDS"**
EndSection
```

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-07
The "intel" driver is probably still incompatible with Xinerama (as it was under fiesty). Try switching to the "i810" driver.

Unfortunately, this may also mean that you need to use 915resolution to get higher resolutions on the screen attached to the intel card.

If you still don't succeed, try switching to an xrandr implementation. Good instruction for this approach are here:

[http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12)

---

### Post by gerdt on 2007-11-08
> **islandwebs said:**
> Section "Device"
Identifier "0 Matrox Graphics, Inc. MGA G400/G450"
Option "DDCMODE" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" **"primary monitor,secondary monitor"**
Boardname "mga"
Busid "PCI:3:0:0"
Driver "mga"
Screen 0
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier "1 Matrox Graphics, Inc. MGA G400/G450"
Option "DDCMODE" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" **"primary monitor,secondary monitor"**
Boardname "mga"
Busid "PCI:3:0:0"
Driver "mga"
Screen 1
EndSection

You have to replace **"primary monitor,secondary monitor"** by what type of monitor it is:

> ```
Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" #replace primary monitor with the following:
Ati Driver:
NONE   -- Not connected
CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

i810 Driver:
CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
LFP -- Local Flat Panel
DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
TV
CRT2
LFP2
DFP2
TV2
NONE -- Not connected
```

---

### Post by urukrama on 2007-11-10
I am unable to set this up with my laptop and an extra screen. I run Ubuntu Dapper, and have an **ATI Technologies, Inc. Rage Mobility P/M AGP 2x** graphic card that came with my laptop (Compaq Presario 1028 ).

I've followed all the steps as explained in the howto, but when I restart x, it tells me that the two devices mentioned in the device section "may not be assigned to the same adapter".

Does this mean I won't be able to set up a second screen? Or is there some work-around, or something I did wrong?

(I ran the "lspci -X | grep VGA" command just to make sure, and it list only one graphic card, as I expected)

I searched the forums and the internet, but nothing came up. Help is appreciated :)

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-11
> **islandwebs said:**
> I am running Gutsy with a desktop PC.  It has run up to five monitors in the past using Windows XP but I now just want to run two with Ubunutu.


gutsy works best with xrandr rather than xinerama.  There's a complete description on this page:

[http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12)

xrandr helps you identify your monitors and resolutions so that you can modify the xorg.conf correctly.

---

### Post by krisfrajer on 2007-11-12
Hi
Now I have tried all the tips before to set up my radeon9600XT but it did not work. I tried through the xinerama way and I could not get both monitors working together in the same session without having a "low resolution mode" at the beginning of the session. However I tried the xrandr --auto and I believe the situation is better than before. Now I have both monitors working at the same time but They are in clone mode.

I noticed through xrandr --verbose that both screens have different refresh rates and different CRTC. I do not know if they have to be in the same mode to deactivate the clone mode. Anyways, how can I get around to deactivate the clone mode?

One more thing, the mode of the monitors is said to be configure in 1024x768 mode however I have the impression my resolution is smaller that what it is reported. I know that because of the size of the icons. I just wanted to point this out.

Thanks for any advice to get my two monitors working.
Kfj

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-12
krisfrajer: here's my suggestion:

Follow the xrandr Howto procedure:

Clear out the unwanted stuff from xorg.conf
Just leave the virtual line in the *one* screen section - and make sure it's big enough!
use xrandr at the command line to set up your two screens manually
once it's working, make the setup permanent in xorg.conf.

---

### Post by krisfrajer on 2007-11-13
I will start from scratch. About the screen size... how big is enough?
I though the configuration in the command line affected the xorg.cong file immediately. Doesnt it?
Kfj

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-14
> **krisfrajer said:**
> I will start from scratch. About the screen size... how big is enough?
I though the configuration in the command line affected the xorg.cong file immediately. Doesnt it?
Kfj

virtual screen size needs to be big enough to contain both screens, so it depends on your screen size.  1400x1050 plus 1280x1024 side by side means 1400+1280 x max(1050,1024) or 2680x1050.

---

### Post by Cynikiss on 2007-11-19
Okay, I've followed the instructions from the OP very carefully, and I'm not sure what I'm still doing wrong here. I'm running two separate nVidia cards, (GeForce 6200 AGP and GeForce 4400 PCI) each with their own separate monitor. I realize that for dual displays with nVidia cards, TwinView is usually the way to go, but from how I understand it, that only works if you're running dual monitors from ONE card; whereas I'm attempting to run dual monitors from two separate cards, which is why I'm attempting to do this with Xinerama. If that's incorrect, please point me in the right direction. I would greatly appreciate it!

If setting this up with Xinerama is the correct way to go about it, I guess I need some assistance with my xorg.conf file. Below, I've included my xorg.conf file's contents. I had followed the original steps very carefully, but perhaps I missed something anyway. Any advice folks could provide would be much appreciated. :)

In case it makes any difference: I used Envy to install the drivers for both video cards. (I had trouble trying to install the drivers provided on the nVidia site manually.)

> # nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Feb 26 23:38:46 PST 2007

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen         0 "Main Screen"
Screen	1	"Second Screen" LeftOf "Main Screen"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"

EndSection

    Option         "CorePointer"

# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection


Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"	"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Failsafe Device"
	Boardname	"vesa"
	Busid		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Driver		"vesa"
	Screen	0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Failsafe Monitor"
	Vendorname	"Plug 'n' Play"
	Modelname	"Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Failsafe Device"
	Monitor		"Failsafe Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
  screen 1 "screen1" leftof "Default Screen"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Inputdevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection
Section "device" # 
	Identifier	"device1"
	Boardname	"vesa"
	Busid		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Driver		"vesa"
	Screen	1
EndSection
Section "screen" # 
	Identifier	"screen1"
	Device		"device1"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Monitor		"monitor1"
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1024x768@75"	"1024x768@70"	"832x624@75"	"1024x768@60"	"800x600@60"	"1280x960@60"	"800x600@75"	"800x600@72"	"800x600@56"	"640x480@75"	"640x480@72"	"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "monitor" # 
	Identifier	"monitor1"
	Vendorname	"Generic CRT Display"
	Modelname	"Monitor 1024x768"
	Horizsync	31.5-61.0
	Vertrefresh	50-75
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@72" 31.5 640 664 704 832 480 489 491 520 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@75" 31.5 640 656 720 840 480 481 484 500 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@72" 50.0 800 856 976 1040 600 637 643 666 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@75" 49.5 800 816 896 1056 600 601 604 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "832x624@75" 57.284 832 864 928 1152 624 625 628 667 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
	Option		"Xinerama"	"true"
EndSection


I know this doesnt have the changes in it that were required, but this is what the file reverted to after the restart and i dont know what to change now.

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-19
Cynikiss: So what happens?  I can't see anything obviously wrong with that xorg.conf but without knowing your symptoms it's difficult to know where to look.

Xivanari: Unless you saved a backup xorg.conf you are going to need to start from scratch.  What version of Ubuntu are you running and what are you trying to achieve?

### Post by Cynikiss on 2007-11-21
Well, that's the strange thing.... nothing happens. The main monitor works fine, like it always has, and the second monitor does nothing. I've checked the Device Manager, and the second card is recognized, so it's not that the system isn't seeing it. I've made sure all the hardware connections are firm and all that, so it's not just me being a bonehead and having the monitor unplugged or something. So... I'm at a loss.

### Post by PopeZaphod on 2007-11-21
I'm running an OptiPlex GX620 with Intel graphics and a PCI card that extends the built-in graphics to a second graphics port (DVI) in addition to the built-in VGA connector.  After a lot of reading and Googling I managed to get dual displays working in a fashion.  Each display is unique, but they both have their own separate Gnome panels and I can't drag running applications between screens.  If I mouse over from one screen to the other, the active window changes.  If I drag a file from one desktop to the other it looks like I'm copying the file over.  Am I running two individual X sessions?  Is there a way to have one session cover both screens?  I'm guessing I need to add a virtual screen setting somewhere in my xorg.conf file, but I'm not exactly sure where.  Any advice would be appreciated.

Here's my xorg.conf file:

```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
Identifier "Multihead layout"
Screen 0 "Screen0" 0 0
Screen 1 "Screen1" LeftOf "Screen0"
InputDevice "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
InputDevice "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
Option "Xinerama" "off"
Option "Clone" "off"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection


Section "Module"
	Load		"extmod"
	Load		"fbdevhw"
	Load		"record"
	Load		"freetype"
	Load		"type1"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"dri"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Keyboard0"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Mouse0"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection



Section "Monitor"
Identifier "Monitor0"
VendorName "Monitor Vendor"
ModelName "Dell 1704FPV (Analog)"
DisplaySize 340 270
HorizSync 30.0 - 81.0
VertRefresh 56.0 - 76.0
Option "dpms"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
Identifier "Monitor1"
VendorName "Monitor Vendor"
ModelName "Dell 1704FPV (Analog)"
HorizSync 30.0 - 81.0
VertRefresh 56.0 - 76.0
Option "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier "Videocard0"
Driver "i810"
VendorName "Videocard vendor"
BoardName "Intel 915"
BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
Screen 0
Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,DFP"
EndSection

Section "Device"
Identifier "Videocard1"
Driver "i810"
VendorName "Videocard Vendor"
BoardName "Intel 915"
BusID "PCI:0:2:0"
Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Screen0"
Device "Videocard0"
Monitor "Monitor0"
DefaultDepth 24
SubSection "Display"
Viewport 0 0
Depth 24
Modes "1280x1024" "1280x1024"
EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
Identifier "Screen1"
Device "Videocard1"
Monitor "Monitor1"
DefaultDepth 24
SubSection "Display"
Viewport 0 0
Depth 24
Modes "1280x1024"
EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
Group 0
Mode 0666
EndSection
```

### Post by gerdt on 2007-11-21
```
**Option "Xinerama" "off"**
```
should be on I believe:
```
Option "Xinerama" "true"
```

---

### Post by PopeZaphod on 2007-11-21
Thanks!  That did the trick.:KS

---

### Post by veloce on 2007-11-22
> **PopeZaphod said:**
> Thanks!  That did the trick.:KS

Interestingly, the way you said you had it working before turning on xinerama is not supposed to work under Gutsy, though it did under Fiesty .  (It was my preferred method of setting up my two screens.)

	#	Option		"Xinerama"	"true"
EndSection 

```

Primary is the laptop which has a max res of 1024x768 Secondary is the LCD with a resolution if 1280x1024.  When I'm in X, and I hit Function + CRT/LCD it switches to the Secondary which is correctly set to 1280X1024 with its own desktop.  When I hit the Function +CRT/LCD again... nothing happens.. and the laptop screen is blank.

Again if I change BOTH resolutions to 1024x768 then on the first Function + crt/lcd it switches on the secondary monitor, second press switches back to laptop, third changes the mode to crt+lcd and they both work.

Any suggestions?

---

### Post by gerdt on 2007-12-12
To be honest, I have no experience with laptops and external monitors, only with desktops. I do want to find out, because I also have a laptop, but I haven't had the time to "experiment" with it. So I don't know (yet) how to deal with the Fn + CRT/LCD button. Perhaps we can find out together ;)

Even though, I can give you one suggestion. Of course, first make a backup ;)

In my own monitor sections, I removed all resolution related lines.
Make sure, BTW, that HorizSync and VertRefresh for both monitors respectively, are correct:
```

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "SyncMaster 720N"
        Option          "DPMS"
        HorizSync       30-81
        VertRefresh     56-75
EndSection


```

In my screen sections, I only put the resolutions I wanted, nothing less, nothing more.
Both my monitors run on 1280x1024, so I thought, why tell the machine what resolutions he *might* use, when I **know** for sure that this is the only one the machine should always use?

Both my Screen sections look like this:

```

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      *name*
        Device          *name*
        Monitor         *name*
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Modes           "1280x1024"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I suggest you first put the "1024x768" resolution in both Screen sections, just to make sure at least that is working. When that is working, change the resolution of your secondary screen into "1280x1024".

I don't know if this will work. The main reason I do this in my own conf file, is that I like it to be organized, so I always remove as much irrelevant lines as I can. Second reason was that I had some resolution problems my self in the past, when I was still using Debian instead of Ubuntu. I had the feeling that by removing all the resolutions but the one I wanted to use solved this.

Hope this helps. I must say that I read some things (don't know where), that Xinerama with two different resolutions gives problems sometimes. I hope it doesn't give you problems. If you finally solved your problem please let us know, I'm very interested in your result.

Grtz, Gerard

---

### Post by gerdt on 2007-12-12
> **dholland said:**
> ... The max this laptop monitor will do is 1024x768... 

I just realized (but I could be wrong), that perhaps your external monitor can handle 1280x1024, but your laptops graphical card cannot. You say your primary laptop screen can only handle 1024x768. This primary monitor max is probably related to you graphical max resolution.

I had the same problem with an older laptop I used. The laptop screen died, so untill buying a new laptop, I used the old one connecting it to a external monitor. The problem was that the external monitor was new and could handle 1280, but because the graphical card of the laptop could only handle 1024, I could not set the laptop to 1280 and had to use it with 1024.

So if you're facing the same situation I faced, your graphical card can't support 1280 and therefore you have these problems.

Conclusion: Go to dell.com, find out the specs of your graphical card, and find out *if* it supports 1280x1024. If it doesn't, you know why you're facing these problems and you'll have to live with 1024x768 even though the external monitor can handle more. If the specs say otherwise, you can look at my suggestion in my previous post

---

### Post by dholland on 2007-12-12
Thanks for the responses and the attempt to help... Let me give some more information.

If I set both the laptop and the monitor to 1024X768 as you suggested it does work... I think I mentioned that possibly a few posts ago.  You are correct, that is the max resolution the laptop screen will do - however that is NOT the max resolution capable by the video card.  The laptop is dual boot, Ubuntu and windows.  In windows I have an external monitor at work that I've actually turned up to 1600x1200 - so the video card is more than capable.

I wrote this before but lets see if I can make it clearer.  In order to activate the external monitor in linux (in 1024x768) I change my xorg.conf and make both screens 1024x768... When I boot up and log in only the laptop screen is active.  I push the Function button + the CRT/LCD button on the laptop keyboard and I have to cycle through to get to the correct mode.  The modes each time I push those buttons come in this order:

Laptop Screen is active
FN+CRT/LCD keypress activates External Screen
FN+CRT/LCD keypress activates Laptop Screen
FN+CRT/LCD keypress Activates External Screen + Laptop Screen

So on that third keypress both of the screens are active.

Now the problem comes when I change the External Screen to 1280x1024.. then this happens:


Laptop Screen is active
FN+CRT/LCD keypress activates External Screen  (*Note* and when its activated its 1280x1024)
FN+CRT/LCD keypress does nothing.  Laptop Screen remains blank.

No matter how many times I hit that button the external screen is the only one that remains active.  Also its worth noting that if I disable or # out the Xinerama option (meaning the desktops are now separate and each one has its own menu bar) that when the secondary screen is active in 1280x768 I have a menu bar and am able to open applications and everything works.  

I hope I've provided enough information for the problem to make sense.. well or at least shine light on exactly what I'm experiencing.

### Post by veloce on 2007-12-16
> **dholland said:**
> Thanks for attempting the help.  Hopefully someone will read this and have an idea.

Sorry, I haven't been reading the forums as often as usual recently.  I have a d520 too, and have tried enumerable options. 

If you want to use two monitors to give an extended desktop under Ubuntu 7.10, the best idea is to use xrandr.  With that, you set up a virtual desktop in your xorg.conf, say 2048x768  for two 1024x768 screens side by side, then use xranr to specify where to place each of the physical screens within it.

See this guide: [http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12](http://wiki.debian.org/XStrikeForce/HowToRandR12)

To get 3d working, I have sacrificed the extended desktop and have ended up attaching the second screen to an old "scrap" machine and I'm using synergy to use the same keyboard and mouse across the two systems.  I use the old machine to rdp to (virtual) windows machines either on my laptop or elsewhere on the work network.

### Post by anfractuosities on 2007-12-20
Ok so i got it working, kinda.  I installed grandr and added "option "grandr" "true" instead of "xinerama" 

ONly one problem. I cannot extend my windows onto the other monitor. though I can dnd.  So im gonna try n figa that out


SOLVED

I edited this line out of my xorg.conf
```

Option "EnablePrivateBackZ" "yes" #Enable 3d support <= May Not Work
```

and it works fine

---

### Post by Ratham on 2007-12-22
Hi all,

I've been trying a LOT of different things and I just can't get my current install to work with dual monitors.  Gutsy 7.10, Gnome Version 2.20, Motherboard is an abit LG-95Z, graphics are onboard Intel and I added an older GeForce FX 5200 (128 MB RAM) PCI video card.  By tweaking XORG.CONF I can get either card & attached monitor to work as long as I keep this line (#113):

Option "Xinerama" "On"

commented out.  However, if I uncomment that line I get a black screen only.  If I wait around 20 seconds I get a message "Ubuntu is running in low-graphics mode", and then if I click Shut Down from there the system hangs.

	Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,NONE"
#End Mods for Dual Monitor part 1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Samtron 75V"
	Modelname	"Custom 1"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@72" 50.0 800 856 976 1040 600 637 643 666 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Intel Corporation 82945G/GZ Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Monitor		"Samtron 75V"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
#Begin Mods for Dual Monitor part 4
#This used to be:
#		Virtual	1024	768
		Virtual	2048	768
#End Mods for Dual Monitor part 4
		Modes		"1024x768@60"	"800x600@60"	"800x600@72"	"800x600@56"	"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
#Begin Mods for Dual Monitor part 3
#This used to be:
#  screen 0 "screen1" 0 0
	screen "Default Screen"
	screen "screen1"  RightOf "Default Screen"
#End Mods for Dual Monitor part 3
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
#Begin Mods for Dual Monitor part 5
	Option "Xinerama" "On"
#End Mods for Dual Monitor part 5
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection
Section "device" # 
	Identifier	"device1"
	Boardname	"NVIDIA GeForce FX (generic)"
	Busid		"PCI:1:3:0"
	Driver		"nv"
#Begin Mods for Dual Monitor part 3
#This used to be:
#	Screen	0
	Screen	1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,NONE"
#End Mods for Dual Monitor part 3
	Vendorname	"NVIDIA"
EndSection
Section "screen" # 
	Identifier	"screen1"
	Device		"device1"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Monitor		"monitor1"
EndSection
Section "monitor" # 
	Identifier	"monitor1"
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection
```

Any ideas?

---

### Post by gerdt on 2007-12-23
In both your Device sections you've put: 
Option "MonitorLayout" "CRT,NONE"

If I'm not mistaken, you are telling the computer that the second screen has no monitor.

---

### Post by Ratham on 2007-12-30
Thanks, but I just got back in town, tried it, and it did NOT work.  Same black screen and eventually "Low graphics mode" message after a reboot.  If anyone has some other ideas I'd certainly appreciate them!

---

### Post by Ratham on 2008-01-08
FYI, I gave up on my old configuration and just bought a new mobo with a PCI Express x16 slot and a new Nvidia card.  Dual monitors are working beautifully with TwinView for me now!

---

### Post by puneit on 2008-01-12
First things first.... Thanks a ton Ziox for such a great Howto
I have successfully attached a Sony LCD V Series 32" TV to my laptop. But you still need to have a look at my xorg.conf - Mouse pointer is not appearing in the external display. I wanted to clone my display so, I just removed the code RightOf "Main Screen" from your Howto. I got the display alright, just not the mouse pointer. So here goes the Xorg,conf

```
Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"	"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies Inc M22 [Mobility Radeon X300]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen 0
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,secondary monitor"
EndSection
Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies Inc M22 [Mobility Radeon X300]"
	Driver		"fglrx"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen 1
	Option "DDCMode" "True"
	Option "MonitorLayout" "LVDS,secondary monitor"

EndSection
Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Laptop Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"External Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"0 ATI Technologies Inc M22 [Mobility Radeon X300]"
	Monitor		"Laptop Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"1 ATI Technologies Inc M22 [Mobility Radeon X300]"
	Monitor		"External Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1024x768"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  	Screen 0 "Main Screen"
	Screen 1 "Second Screen" 
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	Inputdevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"

	Option "Xinerama" "true"

EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection
Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection
```

One more slight problem is there. Though everything else is displaying OK, expect the fonts. Towards the right end of the fonts I can see a faint smear. I can certainly live with it as I just want to watch some movies on the TV. So its fine by me if we don't resolve these...

---

### Post by rnjn.sinha on 2008-01-14
Thanks for this gr8 howto. It at least provided me enough impetus to get started. 
However I facing some problems. Here is my setup

Intel 865 === onboard ==== connected to DELL E73S mointor

ATI 3D Rage LT Pro ==== PCI === connected to IBM E74 monitor

I have to set onboard controller as primary display controller otherwise things don't work.

Now initially my xorg.conf was setup like following. I have removed several less interesting sections

```

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"DELL E773s"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	30-65
	VertRefresh	50-75
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Intel Corporation 82865G Integrated Graphics Controller"
	Monitor		"DELL E773s"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

```

I can see the display on DELL monitor and everything is fine. Now I replaced this file with the following 

```

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ati"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen	0
EndSection


Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel"
	Driver		"i810"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Screen 1
EndSection

	Identifier	"IBM 6332 E74"
	Option		"DPMS"

	Identifier	"DELL E73S"
	Option		"DPMS"

		Modes	"1152x864"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen 0	"Default Screen"
	Screen 1	"Second  Screen"  LeftOf "Default Screen"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

```

Notice the error 

**(EE) Screen 1 deleted because of no matching config section.**

What could be the reason for this error? Thanks in advance

---

### Post by veloce on 2008-01-15
> **puneit said:**
> 
I wanted to clone my display ...

```


	Option "Xinerama" "true"


```



To clone the display you need to set Xinerama to false and, for paranoia's sake add:

	Option "Clone" "true"

---

### Post by rnjn.sinha on 2008-01-16
> **rnjn.sinha said:**
> 
Notice the error 

**(EE) Screen 1 deleted because of no matching config section.**

What could be the reason for this error? Thanks in advance

I managed to fix the above error by not specifying Screen in Device Section. The X starts all right but instead of login screen I now see a message box about greeter application crashing. If I click on OK button then the message keeps reappearing.

I can move my mouse cursor to both monitors. So it seems that the Xinerama is working fine but now I am stuck with this crash. I am using Xubuntu 7.10. Can anybody help me on this.


Thanks in advance

---

### Post by pimpaulogy on 2008-01-16
I think I am in a similar boat that rnjn.sinha is in.

Here's my story:

1 onboard Intel Video Card (primary)
1 nVidia PCIx card (secondary)

2 Viewsonic LCD monitors (VP730b and VP171b)  Very similar to each other

So, I went through and setup the Xorg.conf file as instructed.  After tweaking and retweaking and resetting up the file, I believe I have all the device particulars correct.

In each "Device" section, I found that each setting for Screen had to be 0, or I would only see the a display on the monitor connected to the Screen 0 device.

Understand that I have tweaked and tweaked the settings to get to this point and I feel that I am really close.

However, I do have to comment out the Option "Xinerama" "true" line in the ServerLayout section or I too get the error message about Low Grade graphics and wanting me to either configure it there or continue with the Low Grade settings.

So, with that commented out and both Devices set to Screen 0, I finally get both screens to light up at log in.  Here's the catch, I can move my mouse pointer from the RightScreen (main) to the LeftScreen, but once the mouse it over there, I can't move it back, no matter if I try to run the pointer of the top, bottom, left, or right of the screen.

I restart GDM and just log in without moving the pointer to the LeftScreen.  Both screens light up completely with desktop screens on both, but they are different.  The one on the Right (main) has the updates that I made to the toolbar, but the one on the Left is "stock".  Again, I move my point over to the Left and I am once again stuck.

I guess at this point I am running two instances of GDM.  What I want to do is span one instance across both monitors.

Can anyone take a look at my xorg.conf file and point me in the right direction?

Thanks.

```

	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  	Screen 0 "LeftScreen" 0 0
	Screen 1 "RightScreen" RightOf "LeftScreen"
#	Option "Xinerama" "true"
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection



Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

```

### Post by pimpaulogy on 2008-01-17
Well, I guess I fixed mine.

I still don't have the ability to span one desktop across both screens, but that doesn't really bother me.  I can copy and paste from one screen to the other and that's all I really wanted.

So, what fixed it for me?  That is the ridiculous part.  One little line of text that should work was breaking the whole thing.

In the ServerLayout section, I had this line of code:
```
Screen 1 "RightScreen" RightOf "LeftScreen"
```

Which just means that my screen called "RightScreen" is to the Right of the screen called "LeftScreen" and it does this by using the command RightOf.  When I first looked at it, it appeared to work as designed.  I had a desktop on both screens and I could move my pointer from the LeftScreen to the RightScreen.  Unfortunately, there is a "bug" or something because once I moved the pointer to the RightScreen, I couldn't move it back.  I talked about that in my initial post.

Well, I found the fix.  Apparently the RightOf command is buggy, but the LeftOf command isn't.  I replaced RightOf with LeftOf (and physically switched my monitors positions) and it works like a charm.  I can move the pointer back and forth with no problems.

Xinerama still doesn't work, but like I said, I don't really need that to work, just two working desktops.  FYI: When I enable Xinerama, I get the Low Graphics error.

Hopefully this helps somebody else out there.

---

### Post by Horza on 2008-01-29
> **gerdt said:**
> 
....
So my advice is to experiment. Make step by step, small modifications of your xorg.conf, and use the Ctrl. Alt. Backspace to check whether your new configuration is working or not.

My experience is that Ctrl.Alt.Backspace doesn't work very reliably; what often happens is that X can't restart properly and replaces your xorg.conf file with a minimalistic lo res one.  Various other methods of restarting X seem unreliable, at least in my somewhat klutsy hands, so I'd recommend just doing a full reboot.

Otoh, gerdt's advice to use fglrx with 9600's seems spot-on to me, that is, it worked where various other things failed.

---

### Post by miesnerd on 2008-02-12
hey guys-
Used the how- to but now I have two monitors that are exactly the same (as opposed to being two different desktops. Oh, and its low res.. I assume that's because its confused.
if it makes any difference, the first monitor is a basic X2gen 19" monitor (vga) the second is a x2gen 19" widescreen connected via HDMI.

Here's the xorg.conf

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"0 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X1250"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
	  Screen	 0
Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" #replace primary monitor with the following:
Ati Driver:
NONE   -- Not connected
CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

i810 Driver:
CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
LFP -- Local Flat Panel
DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
TV
CRT2
LFP2
DFP2
TV2
NONE -- Not connected

EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"1 ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X1250"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
    	  Screen	 0
Option "DDCMode" "True"
Option "MonitorLayout" "primary monitor,secondary monitor" #replace primary monitor with the following:
Ati Driver:
NONE   -- Not connected
CRT   -- Analog CRT monitor
TMDS   -- Desktop flat panel
LVDS   -- Laptop flat panel

i810 Driver:
CRT -- Cathode Ray Tube
LFP -- Local Flat Panel
DFP -- Digital Flat Panel
TV
CRT2
LFP2
DFP2
TV2
NONE -- Not connected

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"First Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Second Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection


Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Main Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"VA1912wSERIE"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1440x1440" "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Second Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"VA1912wSERIE"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1440x1440" "1440x900" "1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen 	0	"Main Screen"
	Screen	1	'Second Screen Leftof "Main Screen"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"

# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection



Miesnerd

### Post by shieldw0lf on 2008-02-13
Hey everyone,

I've been working with headless Ubuntu LAMP servers from the command line for a while, but I'm rather new to the desktop scene.  I'd like to get off Windows completely, and I'm not really sure how to get started getting my multi-head setup working again.

I'm using a Gateway GT5238E Media Center PC, which has integrated video that I haven't been using.  It supports DVI and VGA.

I've got a GeForce 7100 GS PCIe card, which supports DVI, VGA and component video, and I've got a 17" CRT attached to the VGA port and a TV attached to the component video output.

I've also got an ATI Rage 128 PRO PCI card connected, and a second 17" CRT attached to its VGA port.

I've installed Gutsy, and it's currently showing a single display on the monitor attached to the Geforce 7100.

I'd like to get my two monitors and television working again, and I'm rather confused as to what approach I should be taking, or if I might be better off to ditch the PCI card and use the integrated video for my second monitor, or what.  I'd rather not be obligated to try and find a DVI-to-VGA adapter before I get this working, I'd have to get one mailed in.  Which seems to rule out the Twinview approach at least in the short term.

If someone could point me in the direction of the right approach, I'd appreciate it.

Cheers,
SW

	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY

Section "Device"

	VertRefresh	50-160
EndSection

		Modes	"1440x900@75"	
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"aticonfig-Screen[1]"
	Device		"aticonfig-Device[1]"
	Monitor		"aticonfig-Monitor[1]"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1280x1024@75"	
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
	Option		"Composite"	"0"
EndSection


```
